modern_context_en: Mandukya Upanishad describes three states: Jagrat (waking), Svapna (dreaming), Sushupti (deep sleep). Devi presides over the dream state. In dreams we are creator, actor, and audience simultaneously. A good dream may be Devi's message. Start dream journaling; often dreams reveal future events or solutions.
nri_context: When recurring dreams abroad about India, your childhood home, or departed loved ones carry messages you cannot ignore, Svapnatī Devi is communicating through your dream-state, transcending the distance your waking mind cannot.
usage_context: Chant before sleep for divine dreams, during Mandukya Upanishad study, or during dream-analysis practice.

meaning_en: She who is the essence of Taijasa, the luminous self that experiences dreams
meaning_hi: तैजस (स्वप्न में अनुभव करने वाली प्रकाशमयी आत्मा) का सार

taijasa.en: Taijasa (the luminous dreamer-self; the consciousness that illuminates dreams from within; Mandukya Upanishad term)
taijasa.hi: तैजस (स्वप्न-द्रष्टा; भीतर से स्वप्न प्रकाशित करने वाली चेतना; मांडूक्य उपनिषद)

modern_context: मांडूक्य उपनिषद में जाग्रत अवस्था का आत्मा 'विश्व' है, स्वप्न अवस्था का 'तैजस', सुषुप्ति का 'प्राज्ञ'। तैजस = तेज (light) से बना, अर्थात् सपनों में जो प्रकाश है वह बाहरी सूर्य से नहीं, भीतरी तैजस से आता है। जब सपने में रंग दिखें, वह तैजस प्रकाश है। देवी उस तैजस की आत्मा हैं।
modern_context_en: In Mandukya Upanishad, the waking state's self is 'Vishva', dream state's 'Taijasa', deep sleep's 'Prajna'. Taijasa comes from Tejas (light), meaning the light in dreams comes not from the external sun but from inner Taijasa. When colours appear in dreams, that is Taijasa light. Devi is the soul of that Taijasa.
nri_context: When vivid, colourful dreams abroad illuminate solutions to problems your waking mind could not solve, the inner light of Taijasātmikā is processing and illuminating from within.
usage_context: Chant during Mandukya Upanishad study, dream-consciousness meditation, or when exploring the nature of dreams.

meaning_en: She who is the deep sleep state where individual awareness merges into cosmic rest
meaning_hi: सुषुप्ति (गहन निद्रा) अवस्था जहाँ व्यक्तिगत चेतना ब्रह्माण्डीय विश्राम में लीन होती है

suptā.en: the deep-sleep state; Sushupti Avastha where individual consciousness rests in undifferentiated awareness
suptā.hi: सुषुप्ति अवस्था; व्यक्तिगत चेतना अविभेदित जागरूकता में विश्राम करती है

modern_context: सुषुप्ति (deep sleep) में न स्वप्न होता है, न जागृति, केवल शान्ति। जब सुबह उठकर कहें 'बहुत अच्छी नींद आई', वह सुषुप्ति का अनुभव था। Ayurveda कहता है 'निद्रा सुखं भवति', नींद सुखदायी है। WHO कहता है 7-9 घण्टे नींद ज़रूरी। देवी सुप्ता गहरी नींद की शक्ति हैं। Insomnia से पीड़ित लोग इस नाम का जाप करें।
modern_context_en: In Sushupti (deep sleep) there are no dreams, no waking, only peace. When you wake saying 'I slept wonderfully', that was Sushupti's experience. Ayurveda says 'Nidra Sukham Bhavati', sleep is blissful. WHO recommends 7-9 hours sleep. Devi Supta is the power of deep sleep. Those with insomnia should chant this name.
nri_context: When jet lag, work stress, or homesickness abroad steal your sleep, invoking Suptā Devi before bed invites the deep, restorative rest your body and mind desperately need.
usage_context: Chant before sleep, during insomnia treatment, or during Mandukya Upanishad Sushupti study. Also during Yoga Nidra.

meaning_en: She who is the essence of Prajna, the wise awareness that persists even in deep sleep
meaning_hi: प्राज्ञ (सुषुप्ति में भी बनी रहने वाली बुद्धिमान जागरूकता) का सार

prājña.en: Prajna (the wise knower in deep sleep; the consciousness that continues even when mind stops; Mandukya Upanishad term)
prājña.hi: प्राज्ञ (सुषुप्ति में जागरूक; मन रुकने पर भी बनी रहने वाली चेतना)

modern_context: मांडूक्य उपनिषद की तीन अवस्थाओं का trilogy पूर्ण: विश्व (जाग्रत, 777-जागरिणी), तैजस (स्वप्न, 778-779), प्राज्ञ (सुषुप्ति, 780-781)। प्राज्ञ वह awareness है जो deep sleep में भी 'जानती' है। इसीलिए सुबह उठकर कह सकते हैं 'मैं सोया था', क्योंकि कोई तो जाग रहा था जो सोने का record रख रहा था। वह प्राज्ञ देवी हैं।
modern_context_en: Mandukya Upanishad's three-state trilogy completes: Vishva (waking, 777-Jagarini), Taijasa (dreaming, 778-779), Prajna (deep sleep, 780-781). Prajna is the awareness that 'knows' even in deep sleep. That is why you can say upon waking 'I slept well'; someone was awake recording the sleep. That someone is Prajna Devi.
nri_context: The fact that you can recall 'I slept peacefully last night' despite being unconscious during sleep proves Prājñātmikā was silently witnessing your rest, a faithful guardian who never clocks out.
usage_context: Chant during Mandukya Upanishad study, when contemplating the witness in deep sleep, or during consciousness-studies. Completes the three-state trilogy.

meaning_en: She who is Turiya, the fourth state of consciousness transcending waking, dreaming, and deep sleep
meaning_hi: तुरीय, चौथी चेतना-अवस्था जो जाग्रत, स्वप्न और सुषुप्ति से परे है

turyā.en: Turiya (the fourth state; pure consciousness that pervades and transcends all three states; the silent witness behind waking, dreaming, and sleeping)
turyā.hi: तुरीय (चौथी अवस्था; तीनों अवस्थाओं में व्याप्त और उनसे परे शुद्ध चेतना; मौन साक्षी)

modern_context: तुरीय मांडूक्य उपनिषद का सबसे महत्त्वपूर्ण सिद्धान्त है। जाग्रत, स्वप्न, सुषुप्ति तीन अवस्थाएँ बदलती हैं लेकिन तुरीय नहीं बदलता। वह तीनों में present है और तीनों से परे भी। जैसे cinema screen पर scenes बदलते हैं लेकिन screen नहीं बदलता, वैसे ही तीन अवस्थाएँ बदलती हैं लेकिन तुरीय (देवी) स्थिर है।
modern_context_en: Turiya is Mandukya Upanishad's most important concept. Waking, dreaming, deep sleep change but Turiya does not. It is present in all three and beyond all three. Like scenes change on a cinema screen but the screen remains, three states change but Turiya (Devi) is unchanging.
nri_context: Through all the changing states of immigrant life abroad, the excited waking, the anxious dreaming, the exhausted sleeping, one thing remains constant: the Turyā awareness witnessing every transition.
usage_context: Chant during Turiya meditation, Mandukya Upanishad Chapter 7 study, or when seeking the unchanging behind the ever-changing. The most philosophically significant consciousness name.

meaning_en: She who transcends all states of consciousness altogether
meaning_hi: सभी चेतना-अवस्थाओं से पूर्णतः परे

avasthā.en: states (waking, dreaming, deep sleep, and even Turiya)

modern_context: यह तुरीय से भी ऊपर है: तुरीयातीत (beyond Turiya)। जब कोई पूछे 'आप किस अवस्था में हैं?', देवी कहें 'किसी में नहीं और सबमें।' यह सबसे गहन paradox है। जैसे आकाश में बादल आते-जाते हैं लेकिन आकाश किसी अवस्था में नहीं, वैसे ही देवी में अवस्थाएँ आती-जाती हैं लेकिन देवी किसी अवस्था में नहीं।
modern_context_en: This is beyond even Turiya: Turiyatita (beyond the fourth). When asked 'what state are you in?', Devi says 'in none and in all.' This is the deepest paradox. Like clouds come and go in the sky but the sky is in no state, states come and go in Devi but Devi is in no state.
nri_context: When you reach a place abroad where you are no longer 'adjusting', 'settling', or 'thriving', when all labels for your state dissolve into simply being, you touch the sarvāvasthāvivarjitā reality.
usage_context: Chant during the highest non-dual meditation, Turiyatita contemplation, or when all states and categories dissolve into pure being.

meaning_en: She who is the creator of the entire universe
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण सृष्टि की रचयिता
modern_context: ब्रह्मा सृष्टिकर्ता कहलाते हैं लेकिन असली सृष्टिकर्त्री देवी हैं। ब्रह्मा को शक्ति कहाँ से मिली? देवी से। जब कोई स्त्री बच्चे को जन्म दे, वह सृष्टिकर्त्री है। जब कोई entrepreneur company शुरू करे, artist painting बनाए, या writer novel लिखे, सब सृष्टिकर्त्री शक्ति से। पाँच cosmic functions (784-788) की series शुरू।
modern_context_en: Brahma is called the creator but the real creator is Devi. Where did Brahma get his power? From Devi. When a woman births a child, she is Srishtikatri. When an entrepreneur starts a company, artist makes a painting, or writer creates a novel, all through Srishtikatri Shakti. The five cosmic functions series (784-788) begins.
nri_context: Every new venture you create abroad, a business, a home, a community, a family, channels Sṛṣṭikartrī's creative power through your hands into the material world.
usage_context: Chant when creating anything new, during Brahma puja, or when celebrating creative power. First of the five cosmic functions.

meaning_en: She who takes the form of Brahma to perform creation
meaning_hi: सृष्टि करने के लिए ब्रह्मा का रूप धारण करने वाली
modern_context: देवी ने सृष्टि के लिए ब्रह्मा का रूप लिया। अर्थात् ब्रह्मा देवी की ही अभिव्यक्ति हैं। जब architect building design करे, वह ब्रह्मरूपा में है। जब programmer code लिखे, scientist hypothesis बनाए, या chef नई recipe create करे, सब ब्रह्मरूपा शक्ति। Create करना divine act है।
modern_context_en: Devi took Brahma's form for creation. Brahma is Devi's own expression. When an architect designs a building, she is in Brahmarupa. When a programmer writes code, a scientist forms a hypothesis, or a chef creates a new recipe, all is Brahmarupa Shakti. Creating is a divine act.
nri_context: When you build something from nothing abroad, a startup from a garage, a community from strangers, a home from an empty apartment, you are Brahmarūpā expressing through human hands.
usage_context: Chant during creative work, Brahma puja, or when honouring the creator within. Second of the five cosmic functions.

meaning_en: She who is the protector and sustainer of all creation
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण सृष्टि की रक्षिका और पालनकर्त्री
modern_context: गोप्त्री = protector। विष्णु पालनकर्ता कहलाते हैं लेकिन उनकी शक्ति (protection power) देवी से आती है। माँ जो बच्चे को बीमारियों से बचाए, army जो देश की रक्षा करे, firewall जो computer को virus से बचाए, सब गोप्त्री शक्ति। UN Peacekeeping force भी गोप्त्री का आधुनिक रूप है।
modern_context_en: Goptri = protector. Vishnu is called the sustainer but his protection power comes from Devi. A mother protecting her child from illness, an army guarding the nation, a firewall protecting a computer, all are Goptri Shakti. UN Peacekeeping is also a modern form of Goptri.
nri_context: When you protect your family abroad through insurance, savings, secure housing, and community networks, that protective instinct channels Goptrī's sustaining power across borders.
usage_context: Chant when seeking protection, during Vishnu puja, or during security and safety prayers. Third of the five cosmic functions.

meaning_en: She who takes the form of Govinda (Vishnu/Krishna) to sustain the world
meaning_hi: संसार का पालन करने के लिए गोविन्द (विष्णु/कृष्ण) का रूप धारण करने वाली
modern_context: गोविन्द कृष्ण का प्रिय नाम है। 'गोविन्द बोलो हरि गोपाल बोलो' सबसे सरल कीर्तन है। देवी गोविन्दरूपिणी कृष्ण का रूप लेकर संसार का पालन करती हैं। Tirupati के Lord Venkateswara भी गोविन्द हैं। जब 'Govinda Govinda!' बोलें, वह देवी का ही आह्वान है। ISKCON का Hare Krishna mantra भी गोविन्द को पुकारता है।
modern_context_en: Govinda is Krishna's beloved name. 'Govinda Bolo Hari Gopal Bolo' is the simplest kirtan. Devi takes Govinda's form to sustain the world. Tirupati's Lord Venkateswara is also Govinda. When saying 'Govinda Govinda!', it is invoking Devi. ISKCON's Hare Krishna mantra also calls upon Govinda.
nri_context: When you chant 'Govinda Govinda' during a Tirupati darshan on an India trip, or join an ISKCON kirtan abroad, the name Govinda carries Govindarūpiṇī's sustaining energy through every syllable.
usage_context: Chant during Krishna/Vishnu worship, Tirupati visit, or during 'Govinda' kirtan. Fourth of the five cosmic functions.

meaning_en: She who performs the cosmic dissolution, withdrawing all creation into herself
meaning_hi: ब्रह्माण्डीय संहार करने वाली, सम्पूर्ण सृष्टि को अपने भीतर समेटने वाली

saṃhāriṇī.en: the dissolver, she who withdraws creation; the cosmic recycler
saṃhāriṇī.hi: संहारिणी, सृष्टि को वापस लेने वाली

modern_context: संहार = वापस लेना, नष्ट नहीं। देवी सृष्टि को 'recycle' करती हैं, 'destroy' नहीं। जैसे पेड़ शरद ऋतु में पत्ते गिराता है ताकि वसन्त में नए आएँ, वैसे ही संहार ताकि नई सृष्टि हो। जब पुरानी नौकरी जाए, पुराना रिश्ता ख़त्म हो, पुराना शहर छूटे, सब संहारिणी का recycling है।
modern_context_en: Samhara = withdrawal, not destruction. Devi 'recycles' creation, not 'destroys'. Like a tree sheds leaves in autumn so new ones come in spring, dissolution happens so new creation follows. When an old job ends, an old relationship finishes, an old city is left behind, all is Samharini's recycling.
nri_context: When an entire phase of life abroad ends, closing a business, leaving a city, ending a chapter, Saṃhāriṇī is not destroying but recycling, making space for what comes next.
usage_context: Chant when endings occur, during Rudra puja, or when accepting dissolution as preparation for renewal. Fifth and final of the five cosmic functions.

meaning_en: She who takes the form of Rudra (Shiva) to perform dissolution
meaning_hi: संहार करने के लिए रुद्र (शिव) का रूप धारण करने वाली

rudra.en: Rudra (the fierce aspect of Shiva who performs dissolution; also: one who removes suffering)
rudra.hi: रुद्र (शिव का उग्र रूप; दुःख हरने वाला भी)

modern_context: रुद्र का अर्थ 'रोदयति इति रुद्रः' (जो रुलाए वह रुद्र) और 'रुत् द्रावयति इति रुद्रः' (जो दुःख दूर करे वह रुद्र) दोनों है। देवी रुद्ररूपा दुःख दूर करने के लिए कभी-कभी रुलाती भी हैं। जैसे surgeon काटता है ठीक करने के लिए, वैसे ही रुद्र destroy करता है heal करने के लिए।
modern_context_en: Rudra means both 'one who makes you cry' (Rodayati Iti Rudrah) and 'one who removes suffering' (Rut Dravayati Iti Rudrah). Devi Rudrarupa sometimes makes you cry to remove suffering. Like a surgeon cuts to heal, Rudra destroys to heal.
nri_context: When a painful ending abroad, a lost friendship, a failed venture, a forced move, makes you cry but later proves to be the best thing that happened, Rudrarūpā was performing surgery on your life.
usage_context: Chant during Rudra puja, when painful events prove to be healing, or during Sri Rudram recitation. Also during Mahashivaratri.

meaning_en: She who performs Tirodhana, the divine concealment that hides the truth to enable the cosmic play
meaning_hi: तिरोधान (दिव्य आवरण, सत्य को छिपाना ताकि ब्रह्माण्डीय लीला चले) करने वाली

tirodhāna.en: concealment, veiling, hiding (the fourth cosmic function: concealing divinity so the world-play can happen)
tirodhāna.hi: तिरोधान, आवरण, छिपाना (चौथा ब्रह्माण्डीय कार्य: दिव्यता छिपाना ताकि लीला चले)

modern_context: पाँच cosmic functions में तिरोधान चौथा है: सत्य को छिपाना। क्यों छिपाती हैं देवी? ताकि खेल (लीला) चले। अगर सबको पता हो कि 'मैं ब्रह्म हूँ', तो खोजने का मज़ा ही न रहे। जैसे hide-and-seek में छिपना ज़रूरी है ताकि खोजने का आनन्द हो, वैसे ही तिरोधान ज़रूरी है ताकि आध्यात्मिक खोज का आनन्द हो।
modern_context_en: Among five cosmic functions, Tirodhana is fourth: concealing truth. Why does Devi hide? So the game (leela) continues. If everyone already knew 'I am Brahman', there would be no joy of seeking. Like hide-and-seek requires hiding so finding is joyful, Tirodhana is needed so spiritual seeking is joyful.
nri_context: When Devi seems absent in the hardships of immigrant life, when prayers seem unanswered and faith wavers, Tirodhānakarī is playing the cosmic hide-and-seek that makes the eventual finding infinitely sweeter.
usage_context: Chant when Devi seems hidden, during faith-crisis moments, or when understanding why truth must be veiled for the cosmic game. A profound 790th name completing this batch.

meaning_en: She who is the eternally auspicious one, embodying Sadashiva's grace-bestowing function
meaning_hi: शाश्वत शिवा (मंगलमयी), सदाशिव के अनुग्रह-कार्य का स्वरूप

śivā.en: the auspicious one (feminine); also: embodying the Sadashiva principle of Anugraha/grace)
śivā.hi: शिवा, मंगलमयी; सदाशिव के अनुग्रह सिद्धान्त का स्वरूप

modern_context: पाँच cosmic functions पूर्ण: सृष्टि (784), स्थिति (786-787), संहार (788-789), तिरोधान (790), अनुग्रह (791-सदाशिवा)। सदाशिव पाँचवाँ function 'अनुग्रह' (grace) करते हैं। देवी सदाशिवा वह कृपा हैं जो तिरोधान (छिपाने) के बाद सत्य प्रकट करती हैं। जब लम्बी खोज के बाद उत्तर मिले, वह अनुग्रह है।
modern_context_en: Five cosmic functions complete: Creation (784), Sustenance (786-787), Dissolution (788-789), Concealment (790), Grace (791-Sadashiva). Sadashiva performs the fifth function 'Anugraha' (grace). Devi Sadashiva is the grace that reveals truth after concealment. When after long seeking the answer arrives, that is Anugraha.
nri_context: When years of searching abroad for meaning, purpose, and belonging suddenly crystallize into clarity, that breakthrough is Sadāśivā's anugraha, the grace that lifts the veil after the cosmic hide-and-seek.
usage_context: Chant when grace arrives after long waiting, during Anugraha meditation, or when completing the five cosmic functions series. Also during Sadashiva puja.

meaning_en: She who bestows divine grace, revealing the hidden truth to the seeker
meaning_hi: दिव्य अनुग्रह (कृपा) प्रदान करने वाली, साधक को छिपे सत्य का प्रकटीकरण

anugraha.en: grace, favour, revelation, the fifth cosmic function

modern_context: अनुग्रह पाँचवाँ और सबसे महत्त्वपूर्ण cosmic function है। सृष्टि, स्थिति, संहार, तिरोधान सब अनुग्रह के लिए हैं। जब guru शिष्य को दीक्षा दे, जब साधक को अचानक बोध हो, जब भक्त को दर्शन हों, सब अनुग्रह है। बिना अनुग्रह के मोक्ष सम्भव नहीं। Self-effort + Grace = Liberation।
modern_context_en: Anugraha is the fifth and most important cosmic function. Creation, sustenance, dissolution, concealment all exist for Anugraha. When a guru initiates a disciple, when a seeker has sudden realization, when a devotee has darshan, all are Anugraha. Without grace, moksha is impossible. Self-effort + Grace = Liberation.
nri_context: When a random encounter abroad, a book falling off a shelf, a conversation with a stranger, a song on the radio, triggers a life-changing spiritual insight, that is Anugrahadā's grace choosing its own timing.
usage_context: Chant when seeking grace, during diksha, or when gratitude for grace overwhelms. The crown of the five cosmic functions.

meaning_en: She who is the divine command, the authority behind all cosmic order
meaning_hi: दिव्य आज्ञा, सम्पूर्ण ब्रह्माण्डीय व्यवस्था के पीछे का अधिकार

ājñā.en: command, authority, order; also: Ajna Chakra (the third-eye centre where divine command is received)
ājñā.hi: आज्ञा, आदेश; आज्ञा चक्र (तृतीय नेत्र जहाँ दिव्य आदेश प्राप्त होता है)

modern_context: आज्ञा = command। देवी की आज्ञा से ब्रह्माण्ड चलता है। ग्रह अपनी कक्षा में, ऋतुएँ अपने क्रम में, ज्वार-भाटा अपने समय पर, सब देवी की आज्ञा से। आज्ञा चक्र (third eye) वह स्थान है जहाँ हम देवी की आज्ञा 'receive' करते हैं। Intuition देवी की आज्ञा का subtle form है।
modern_context_en: Ajna = command. The cosmos runs on Devi's command. Planets in orbit, seasons in sequence, tides on time, all by Devi's command. Ajna Chakra (third eye) is where we 'receive' Devi's command. Intuition is the subtle form of Devi's command.
nri_context: When a strong intuition abroad guides you to make a decision that defies logic but proves perfect, that inner knowing came through the Ājñā centre where Devi's command is received.
usage_context: Chant during Ajna Chakra meditation, when following intuition, or when cosmic order is contemplated.

meaning_en: She who is the firm foundation upon which all existence rests
meaning_hi: दृढ़ आधार जिस पर सम्पूर्ण अस्तित्व टिका है
modern_context: प्रतिष्ठा = foundation + honour। मन्दिर में मूर्ति-प्रतिष्ठा (consecration) होती है, समाज में व्यक्ति की प्रतिष्ठा (reputation) होती है। देवी दोनों हैं: ब्रह्माण्ड की नींव भी और उसका सम्मान भी। जब कोई institution विश्वसनीय हो (RBI, Supreme Court, ISRO), उसकी प्रतिष्ठा देवी की कृपा है।
modern_context_en: Pratishtha = foundation + honour. Temples have murti-pratishtha (consecration); people have social pratishtha (reputation). Devi is both: the cosmos's foundation and its honour. When an institution is trustworthy (RBI, Supreme Court, ISRO), its pratishtha is Devi's grace.
nri_context: When your reputation abroad, built over years of integrity, consistent work, and honest dealings, becomes your most valuable asset, that pratiṣṭhā was established by Devi brick by brick.
usage_context: Chant during Prana Pratishtha ceremonies, when seeking stable foundations, or when reputation needs protecting.

meaning_en: She who manifests herself in visible form for the sake of her devotees
meaning_hi: भक्तों के लिए दृश्य रूप में प्रकट होने वाली
modern_context: देवी निराकार हैं लेकिन भक्तों के लिए साकार रूप लेती हैं। जब Ramakrishna Paramahamsa को काली के दर्शन हुए, वह प्रकटाकृति थीं। जब Anandamayi Ma को देवी का vision मिला, वह प्रकटाकृति। जब मन्दिर में मूर्ति अचानक 'जीवित' लगे, आँखें चमकें, मुस्कान दिखे, वह प्रकटीकरण है।
modern_context_en: Devi is formless but takes form for devotees. When Ramakrishna Paramahamsa had Kali's darshan, that was Prakatakruti. When Anandamayi Ma received Devi's vision, that was Prakatakruti. When a temple murti suddenly seems 'alive', eyes sparkling, smile appearing, that is manifestation.
nri_context: When during a deeply emotional puja abroad the murti seems to respond, eyes glistening or a feeling of living presence filling the room, Prakaṭākṛtiḥ is manifesting for your devotion.
usage_context: Chant when seeking Devi's darshan, during Murti puja, or when divine visions are experienced. Also during Ramakrishna Mission events.

meaning_en: She who is the sovereign goddess of all life-breath and vital force
meaning_hi: सभी प्राण (जीवन-वायु, प्राणशक्ति) की ईश्वरी
modern_context: प्राण = life force। बिना प्राण के शरीर शव है। पाँच प्राण (प्राण, अपान, समान, उदान, व्यान) शरीर के अलग-अलग कार्य करते हैं। Pranayama प्राणेश्वरी की सीधी पूजा है। जब COVID में लोगों को साँस लेने में कठिनाई हुई, तब प्राण का मूल्य समझ आया। हर साँस देवी का उपहार है।
modern_context_en: Prana = life force. Without Prana the body is a corpse. Five Pranas (Prana, Apana, Samana, Udana, Vyana) perform different bodily functions. Pranayama is direct worship of Praneshvari. When COVID made breathing difficult, Prana's value was understood. Every breath is Devi's gift.
nri_context: When you practise Pranayama abroad each morning, each conscious breath is a prayer to Prāṇeśvarī who governs the vital force keeping you alive across every timezone.
usage_context: Chant during Pranayama, when breathing feels laboured, or during Prana meditation. Also during respiratory health practices.

meaning_en: She who gives the gift of life-breath to all living beings
meaning_hi: सभी प्राणियों को प्राण (जीवन-वायु) का उपहार देने वाली
modern_context: प्राणदात्री प्राणदान (जीवन देना) करती हैं। जब शिशु जन्म लेकर पहली साँस ले और रोए, उस क्षण प्राणदात्री ने प्राण दिया। जब CPR से किसी की जान बचे, जब ventilator पर साँस चले, जब organ donation से कोई जिए, सब प्राणदात्री का कार्य। Blood donation, organ donation सब प्राणदान है।
modern_context_en: Pranadatri gives Pranadana (the gift of life). When a newborn takes its first breath and cries, Pranadatri gave Prana at that moment. When CPR saves a life, a ventilator supports breathing, or organ donation lets someone live, all are Pranadatri's work. Blood and organ donation are Pranadana.
nri_context: When you register as an organ donor abroad or donate blood regularly, you become a channel for Prāṇadātrī's life-giving power, offering the most sacred gift: the breath of continued life.
usage_context: Chant during birth celebrations, when donating blood/organs, or during life-saving interventions. Also during Prana meditation.

meaning_en: She who manifests as the fifty-one sacred Shakti Peethas across the land
meaning_hi: इक्यावन (51) पवित्र शक्तिपीठों के रूप में प्रकट होने वाली

pañcāśat.en: fifty (also counted as 51 or 52 in different traditions)

pīṭha.en: sacred seat, Shakti Peeth (where parts of Sati's body fell)
pīṭha.hi: पीठ, शक्तिपीठ (जहाँ सती के अंग गिरे)

modern_context: जब सती ने दक्ष यज्ञ में आत्मदाह किया और शिव उनका शव लेकर भटके, विष्णु ने सुदर्शन चक्र से शव के टुकड़े किए। जहाँ-जहाँ अंग गिरे, वहाँ शक्तिपीठ बने: कामाख्या (असम), विशालाक्षी (काशी), हिंगलाज (बलूचिस्तान), जेसोरेश्वरी (बांग्लादेश)। 51 पीठ भारत, नेपाल, बांग्लादेश, पाकिस्तान, श्रीलंका में फैले हैं।
modern_context_en: When Sati immolated herself at Daksha's yajna and Shiva wandered carrying her body, Vishnu cut it with Sudarshana Chakra. Where body parts fell, Shakti Peethas formed: Kamakhya (Assam), Vishalakshi (Kashi), Hinglaj (Balochistan), Jeshoreshwari (Bangladesh). 51 Peethas span India, Nepal, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Sri Lanka.
nri_context: When planning an India trip as a Shakti Peeth pilgrimage, visiting multiple peethas across states, each location where Sati's body touched the earth connects you to Pañcāśatpīṭharūpiṇī's sacred geography.
usage_context: Chant during Shakti Peeth yatra planning, when visiting any Shakti Peeth, or during Sati-Shiva contemplation.

meaning_en: She who is completely free from all chains, the unchained one
meaning_hi: सभी श्रृंखलाओं (बन्धनों) से पूर्णतः मुक्त
modern_context: देवी किसी भी बन्धन में नहीं: न karma का, न time का, न space का, न relationship का। पूर्ण स्वतन्त्र। यह सबसे radical freedom है। जब कोई कहे 'तुम्हें यह करना ही होगा', देवी कहती हैं 'मुझे कुछ नहीं करना'। 'विशृङ्खला' शब्द Women's empowerment का सबसे प्राचीन declaration है: unchained feminine।
modern_context_en: Devi is bound by nothing: not karma, not time, not space, not relationship. Completely free. This is the most radical freedom. When someone says 'you must do this', Devi says 'I must do nothing.' 'Vishrinkhala' is the most ancient declaration of women's empowerment: the unchained feminine.
nri_context: When societal expectations, from India or abroad, try to chain you into a role you have outgrown, Viśṛṅkhalā's unchained spirit gives you divine permission to break free.
usage_context: Chant during liberation practice, when breaking free from constraints, or during Women's Day. The ultimate freedom name.

meaning_en: She who abides in the space of pure discernment and sacred solitude
meaning_hi: शुद्ध विवेक और पवित्र एकान्त के स्थान में विराजमान
modern_context: 800वाँ नाम एक महत्त्वपूर्ण milestone है: 80% यात्रा पूर्ण। विविक्तस्था शान्त एकान्त में रहने वाली हैं। गीता में कृष्ण कहते हैं 'विविक्तसेवी' (एकान्त-प्रिय) साधक श्रेष्ठ है। Digital detox, silent retreat, या रोज़ 15 मिनट phone बन्द करके बैठना, सब विविक्तस्था का अभ्यास है। 800 नामों के बाद शान्ति में बैठने का समय।
modern_context_en: The 800th name is a major milestone: 80% journey complete. Viviktastha abides in quiet solitude. In Gita, Krishna says 'Viviktasevi' (solitude-loving) is the best sadhaka. Digital detox, silent retreats, or 15 minutes daily with phone off, all are Viviktastha practice. After 800 names, time to sit in silence.
nri_context: At this 800th milestone, the teaching is clear: amid the noise of immigrant life abroad, carving out sacred solitude daily is not luxury but necessity. Viviktasthā waits in that silence.
usage_context: Chant during silent retreats, digital detox, or when seeking sacred solitude. A significant 800th milestone marking 80% completion.

meaning_en: She who is the Mother of heroes, who births and inspires valour
meaning_hi: वीरों की माता, शौर्य को जन्म देने और प्रेरित करने वाली
modern_context: जीजाबाई शिवाजी की वीरमाता थीं जिन्होंने बचपन से रामायण-महाभारत की कथाएँ सुनाकर वीर बनाया। पन्ना धाय ने राजकुमार उदय सिंह को बचाने के लिए अपने पुत्र को बलिदान किया। हर माँ जो बच्चों में साहस, ईमानदारी और शौर्य भरे, वह वीरमाता है। वीर केवल युद्धभूमि पर नहीं, परीक्षा-कक्ष में भी होते हैं।
modern_context_en: Jijabai was Shivaji's Veeramata who raised him on Ramayana-Mahabharata stories, creating a warrior. Panna Dhai sacrificed her own son to save Prince Udai Singh. Every mother who fills children with courage, honesty, and valour is Veeramata. Heroes exist not only on battlefields but in exam halls too.
nri_context: When you raise children abroad to be brave, honest, and culturally grounded despite peer pressure to conform, you channel Vīramātā's spirit, birthing heroes who straddle two worlds with courage.
usage_context: Chant during Mother's Day, when honouring courageous mothers, or during Jijabai Jayanti. Also during parenting-focused meditation.

meaning_en: She who gave birth to the sky (Akasha), the mother of space itself
meaning_hi: आकाश (वियत्) को जन्म देने वाली, स्वयं अन्तरिक्ष की माता
modern_context: आकाश (space) सबसे पहले उत्पन्न हुआ, फिर उसमें वायु, अग्नि, जल, पृथ्वी। Space सबको contain करता है। देवी ने space को जन्म दिया, अर्थात् container को contain करने वाली। जब ISRO या NASA अन्तरिक्ष में जाएँ, वे देवी की सन्तान (आकाश) में घूम रहे हैं। Space tourism भी माँ के गर्भ में लौटना है।
modern_context_en: Space (Akasha) was born first, then air, fire, water, earth within it. Space contains everything. Devi birthed space, meaning she contains the container. When ISRO or NASA go to space, they travel within Devi's child (Akasha). Space tourism is returning to the Mother's womb.
nri_context: When flying through the sky between continents, you travel through Viyatprasū's firstborn child. The very space holding your airplane was birthed by Devi.
usage_context: Chant during Akasha Tattva meditation, space-related contemplation, or during ISRO/NASA achievements. Also during Pancha Bhuta meditation.

meaning_en: She who is Mukunda, the bestower of liberation (mukti)
meaning_hi: मुकुन्दा, मुक्ति (मोक्ष) प्रदान करने वाली

mukundā.en: Mukunda (one who grants Mukti/liberation; a name of Vishnu, here applied to Devi)
mukundā.hi: मुकुन्दा (मुक्ति देने वाली; विष्णु का नाम, यहाँ देवी पर लागू)

modern_context: मुकुन्द = मुक्ति + कुन्द (देने वाला)। यह विष्णु का प्रसिद्ध नाम है लेकिन देवी भी मुकुन्दा हैं क्योंकि मोक्ष अन्ततः उन्हीं से मिलता है। 'Mukti' सबसे बड़ा gift है जो कोई दे सकता है: जन्म-मरण के चक्र से मुक्ति। जब साधक 'मुक्त' हो, वह फिर कभी बँधता नहीं। यही परम लक्ष्य है।
modern_context_en: Mukunda = Mukti + Kunda (giver). This is Vishnu's famous name but Devi is also Mukunda because liberation ultimately comes from her. 'Mukti' is the greatest gift anyone can give: freedom from birth-death cycle. When a seeker becomes 'Mukta', they are never bound again. This is the ultimate goal.
nri_context: When the ultimate question abroad shifts from 'how do I succeed here?' to 'how do I become free?', you have turned towards Mukundā, the liberator whose gift surpasses every visa, every green card, every passport.
usage_context: Chant when seeking moksha, during Vishnu puja, or when the ultimate spiritual goal crystallizes.

meaning_en: She who is the abode of liberation, the home where all seeking ends
meaning_hi: मुक्ति का निलय (निवास), जहाँ सब खोज समाप्त होती है
modern_context: मुक्ति का 'घर' देवी हैं। जब कोई 'मुक्त' हो, वह कहाँ जाता है? देवी के पास। मोक्ष destination नहीं, homecoming है। जैसे बच्चा स्कूल से लौटकर माँ की गोद में आता है, वैसे ही आत्मा मुक्त होकर देवी की गोद में लौटती है। 'I want to go home' का spiritual meaning: 'मैं देवी के पास लौटना चाहता हूँ।'
modern_context_en: Devi is the 'home' of Mukti. When someone becomes 'Mukta', where do they go? To Devi. Moksha is not a destination but a homecoming. Like a child returns from school to mother's lap, the soul when liberated returns to Devi's lap. The spiritual meaning of 'I want to go home' is 'I want to return to Devi.'
nri_context: When homesickness abroad reveals itself as a deeper longing not for India but for the ultimate 'home', that yearning is for Muktinilayā, the abode of liberation that is Devi herself.
usage_context: Chant when spiritual homesickness arises, during Moksha meditation, or when contemplating the soul's final homecoming.

meaning_en: She who is the original form from which all divine images derive
meaning_hi: मूल विग्रह (आदि दिव्य रूप) जिससे सभी देवी-मूर्तियाँ उत्पन्न
modern_context: हर मन्दिर में मूलविग्रह (main deity) होती है। तिरुपति में Venkateswara, जगन्नाथपुरी में Jagannath, कामाख्या में Kamakhya, सब मूलविग्रह हैं। लेकिन इन सबकी मूलविग्रह (original source form) देवी ललिता हैं। हर मूर्ति, हर painting, हर photo जो देवी का दर्शन कराए, वह मूलविग्रहरूपिणी का प्रतिबिम्ब है।
modern_context_en: Every temple has a Mulavigraha (main deity). Tirupati's Venkateswara, Jagannathpuri's Jagannath, Kamakhya's Kamakhya, all are Mulavigrahas. But the Mulavigraha (original source form) of all is Devi Lalita. Every murti, painting, or photo showing Devi is a reflection of Mulavigraharupini.
nri_context: When the small Devi photo on your overseas altar is the only divine image you have, this name elevates it: that photo reflects the Mūlavigraha, the original form from which all divine images flow.
usage_context: Chant during Murti puja, temple consecration, or when honouring any divine image as a reflection of the original.

meaning_en: She who knows the innermost feelings and intentions of every being
meaning_hi: हर प्राणी की अन्तरतम भावनाओं और इरादों को जानने वाली
modern_context: देवी शब्द नहीं, भाव (feeling) देखती हैं। जब मन्त्र का उच्चारण ग़लत हो लेकिन भाव सच्चा हो, देवी प्रसन्न होती हैं। जब बच्चा टूटी-फूटी भाषा में 'माँ, I love you' बोले, माँ शब्द नहीं, भाव सुनती है। जब रोते हुए प्रार्थना करें, देवी शब्द नहीं, आँसू देखती हैं। भाव ही सब कुछ है।
modern_context_en: Devi reads not words but bhava (feeling). When mantra pronunciation is wrong but feeling is sincere, Devi is pleased. When a child says 'Ma, I love you' in broken language, the mother hears feeling not words. When praying with tears, Devi sees tears not words. Bhava is everything.
nri_context: When your prayers abroad are in imperfect Sanskrit, when your puja vidhi has gaps, when your Hindi is rusty, Bhāvajñā sees past all imperfections to the sincere feeling underneath. That is all she needs.
usage_context: Chant when feeling unworthy of proper worship, during Bhava-based devotion, or when sincerity matters more than ritual perfection.

meaning_en: She who cures the disease of worldly existence (samsara)
meaning_hi: भवरोग (संसार रूपी बीमारी) का नाश करने वाली

bhava.en: worldly existence, samsara, the cycle of birth and death

modern_context: संसार एक रोग है और देवी उसकी Doctor हैं। रोग के लक्षण: दुःख, चिन्ता, भय, मोह, क्रोध, लालच। दवाई: भक्ति, ज्ञान, वैराग्य। जैसे doctor बीमारी diagnose करता है और इलाज देता है, देवी भवरोग diagnose करती हैं और भक्ति का इलाज देती हैं। Wellness industry $4 trillion की है, लेकिन भवरोग का इलाज free है: देवी-भक्ति।
modern_context_en: Samsara is a disease and Devi is the Doctor. Symptoms: suffering, anxiety, fear, attachment, anger, greed. Medicine: devotion, knowledge, detachment. Like a doctor diagnoses illness and prescribes treatment, Devi diagnoses Bhavaroga and prescribes bhakti. The wellness industry is $4 trillion but Bhavaroga's cure is free: Devi-bhakti.
nri_context: When the 'dis-ease' of immigrant life, perpetual anxiety, identity confusion, existential restlessness, resists every worldly cure, Bhavarogaghnī's prescription of bhakti addresses the root cause that no therapy alone can reach.
usage_context: Chant during spiritual healing, when samsara's suffering peaks, or during Moksha-oriented meditation.

meaning_en: She who sets in motion the wheel of worldly existence
meaning_hi: भवचक्र (संसार-चक्र) को गतिमान करने वाली

pravartinī.en: one who sets in motion, turner

modern_context: पिछला नाम (807) कहता है देवी भवरोग मिटाती हैं, यह नाम कहता है देवी ही भवचक्र चलाती हैं। Paradox: doctor ही बीमारी भी पैदा करे? नहीं, देवी भवचक्र इसलिए चलाती हैं ताकि आत्मा अनुभव करे, सीखे, और अन्ततः मुक्त हो। School कठिन है लेकिन ज़रूरी है graduation के लिए। संसार = school, मोक्ष = graduation।
modern_context_en: Previous name (807) says Devi cures worldly disease; this name says Devi herself runs the worldly cycle. Paradox: the doctor creates the disease? No, Devi runs Bhavachakra so souls experience, learn, and ultimately liberate. School is hard but necessary for graduation. Samsara = school, Moksha = graduation.
nri_context: When the cycle of immigrant life, earn-spend-save-repeat, feels like an endless wheel, this name reveals the purpose: Bhavacakra is Devi's school where every cycle teaches something until you graduate into moksha.
usage_context: Chant during Samsara contemplation, when the cycle feels endless, or during Buddhist-Hindu dialogue on the wheel of existence.

meaning_en: She who is the essence of all Vedic metres and poetic rhythms
meaning_hi: सभी वैदिक छन्दों (काव्य-लय) का सार

chandas.en: Vedic metres, poetic rhythms (Gayatri, Anushtup, Trishtup, Jagati, etc.)
chandas.hi: छन्द, वैदिक काव्य-लय (गायत्री, अनुष्टुप, त्रिष्टुप, जगती आदि)

modern_context: वैदिक छन्द (metres) संस्कृत काव्य का DNA हैं। गायत्री छन्द (24 अक्षर), अनुष्टुप (32 अक्षर), त्रिष्टुप (44 अक्षर)। हर छन्द की एक rhythm है जो brain को specific तरीक़े से प्रभावित करती है। शास्त्रीय संगीत में ताल (rhythm) छन्द का ही विस्तार है। Poetry, rap, song lyrics सब छन्द से जुड़े हैं।
modern_context_en: Vedic metres are Sanskrit poetry's DNA. Gayatri (24 syllables), Anushtup (32), Trishtup (44). Each metre has a rhythm affecting the brain in specific ways. Classical music's Tala (rhythm) is an extension of Chandas. Poetry, rap, song lyrics all connect to Chandas.
nri_context: When the rhythm of a Sanskrit shloka chanted abroad creates a visceral response, goosebumps, tears, or stillness, that is Chandaḥsārā's essence working through the metre's precise acoustic architecture.
usage_context: Chant during Chandas study, poetry composition, or when appreciating Vedic metre. Also during Sama Veda recitation.

meaning_en: She who is the essence of all sacred sciences and scriptures
meaning_hi: सभी पवित्र शास्त्रों और विज्ञानों का सार
modern_context: 14 विद्याएँ (चार वेद, छह वेदांग, न्याय, मीमांसा, पुराण, धर्मशास्त्र) और 64 कलाएँ, सबका सार देवी हैं। जब PhD thesis लिखें, conclusion page सबसे महत्त्वपूर्ण है क्योंकि वह पूरे research का सार है। देवी 14+64 = 78 शास्त्रों का conclusion page हैं। सब पढ़ने के बाद जो बचे: माँ।
modern_context_en: 14 Vidyas (four Vedas, six Vedangas, Nyaya, Mimamsa, Purana, Dharmashastra) and 64 Kalas, the essence of all is Devi. When writing a PhD thesis, the conclusion page is most important because it is the entire research's essence. Devi is the conclusion page of 78 shastras. After reading everything, what remains: Ma.
nri_context: When the vast library of Hindu texts available online abroad creates information overload, this name simplifies everything: Śāstrasārā is the single essence that all those thousands of pages point towards.
usage_context: Chant during scripture study, when seeking the essence behind diverse texts, or when academic study meets spiritual inquiry.

meaning_en: She who is the essence of all sacred mantras ever revealed
meaning_hi: कभी भी प्रकट हुए सभी पवित्र मन्त्रों का सार
modern_context: Chandahsara (809), Shastrasara (810), Mantrasara (811): तीनों 'sara' (essence) names एक trilogy बनाते हैं। छन्द का सार, शास्त्र का सार, मन्त्र का सार, तीनों देवी। जब कोई मन्त्र बोलें, उसका essence (shakti) देवी हैं। ॐ का सार देवी, गायत्री का सार देवी, महामृत्युञ्जय का सार देवी। हर मन्त्र एक wrapper है, अन्दर देवी।
modern_context_en: Chandahsara (809), Shastrasara (810), Mantrasara (811): three 'sara' names form a trilogy. Essence of metres, essence of sciences, essence of mantras, all three are Devi. When chanting any mantra, its essence (shakti) is Devi. Om's essence is Devi, Gayatri's essence is Devi. Every mantra is a wrapper; inside is Devi.
nri_context: When the only mantra you can remember abroad in a crisis is 'Om' or 'Ma', this name validates: you hold the mantrasāra itself. One sincere syllable carries the essence of every mantra ever chanted.
usage_context: Chant during mantra japa, when simplifying practice to one essential mantra, or during Mantra Shastra study. Completes the Sara trilogy.

meaning_en: She who has a slender waist, graceful as a palm tree
meaning_hi: ताड़ (palm tree) के तने जैसी पतली और सुडौल कमर वाली
modern_context: ताड़ (palm tree) लम्बा और पतला होता है। देवी की कमर ताड़ जैसी सुडौल है। भारतीय शिल्पकला में त्रिभंग (three-bend) pose में कमर का curve सबसे सुन्दर माना जाता है। यह शारीरिक वर्णन शरीर को sacred मानने की परम्परा का अंग है। हर शरीर दैवी है, body-shaming अधार्मिक है।
modern_context_en: The palm tree (Tala) is tall and slender. Devi's waist is graceful like a palm tree. In Indian sculpture, the Tribhanga (three-bend) pose considers the waist curve most beautiful. This physical description is part of the tradition of viewing the body as sacred. Every body is divine; body-shaming is irreligious.
nri_context: When beauty standards abroad impose a singular 'ideal' body type, this name celebrates a specific graceful proportion as divine, honouring Indian aesthetic ideals alongside all others.
usage_context: Chant during body-positive meditation, Indian sculpture study, or during Devi's physical-form visualization.

meaning_en: She whose fame is generous and far-reaching, known for magnanimous glory
meaning_hi: उदार (विशालहृदय) कीर्ति (यश) वाली, उदारता के लिए विख्यात
modern_context: देवी की कीर्ति उदार है, अर्थात् उनका यश दान और करुणा के लिए है। Tata, Birla, Azim Premji जैसे दानवीरों की कीर्ति उनके धन से नहीं, उनकी उदारता से है। जब Bill Gates अपनी सम्पत्ति दान करें, वह उदारकीर्ति। असली यश wealth accumulation से नहीं, wealth distribution से आता है।
modern_context_en: Devi's fame is generous, meaning her glory is for giving and compassion. The fame of philanthropists like Tata, Birla, Azim Premji comes not from their wealth but from their generosity. When Bill Gates donates his fortune, that is Udarakirti. True fame comes not from wealth accumulation but from wealth distribution.
nri_context: When your legacy abroad is built not on what you earned but on what you gave back, scholarships funded, community centres built, newcomers mentored, that generous fame is Udārakīrtiḥ's blessing.
usage_context: Chant during philanthropic activities, when seeking noble reputation, or during Daana-focused meditation.

meaning_en: She whose magnificence is unbounded and overflowing
meaning_hi: जिनका वैभव (महिमा) अबाध और उमड़ता हुआ है
modern_context: उद्दाम = unbounded। देवी का वैभव किसी सीमा में नहीं बँधता। Jeff Bezos की सम्पत्ति $150 billion है, लेकिन देवी का वैभव immeasurable है। Milky Way में 100 billion stars हैं और यह एक galaxy है, अरबों galaxies हैं, और यह सब देवी का वैभव है। Economics GDP मापती है लेकिन देवी का GDP (Gross Divine Product) अनन्त है।
modern_context_en: Uddama = unbounded. Devi's magnificence is not bound by limits. Jeff Bezos' wealth is $150 billion but Devi's vaibhava is immeasurable. Milky Way has 100 billion stars and is one galaxy among billions, all Devi's glory. Economics measures GDP but Devi's GDP (Gross Divine Product) is infinite.
nri_context: When the most impressive displays of wealth and power abroad, skyscrapers, tech empires, luxury lifestyles, seem overwhelming, this name reframes them as tiny fractions of Uddāmavaibhavā's unbounded magnificence.
usage_context: Chant when seeking cosmic perspective on worldly glory, during vaibhava meditation, or when human achievements need divine context.

meaning_en: She who manifests as the letters of the sacred alphabet
meaning_hi: पवित्र वर्णमाला (अक्षरों) के रूप में प्रकट होने वाली

varṇa.en: letters, sounds, syllables of the Sanskrit alphabet (also: colours)
varṇa.hi: वर्ण, अक्षर, संस्कृत वर्णमाला के ध्वनि (रंग भी)

modern_context: संस्कृत के 50 अक्षर (16 स्वर + 34 व्यंजन) देवी के शरीर के 50 अंग हैं। 50 शक्तिपीठ 50 अक्षरों से जुड़े हैं। माला (108 मनके) = 50 अक्षर × 2 (शिव-शक्ति) + 8 (अष्ट प्रकृति) = 108। हर अक्षर एक शक्ति है। जब 'अ' बोलें, मुख खुलता है (सृष्टि)। जब 'ह' बोलें, श्वास निकलती है (विसर्ग)।
modern_context_en: Sanskrit's 50 letters (16 vowels + 34 consonants) are 50 parts of Devi's body. 50 Shakti Peethas connect to 50 letters. Mala (108 beads) = 50 letters × 2 (Shiva-Shakti) + 8 (Ashta Prakriti) = 108. Every letter is a Shakti. Saying 'A' opens the mouth (creation). Saying 'Ha' releases breath (dissolution).
nri_context: When teaching your child the Sanskrit alphabet abroad, each letter you trace is an invocation of Varṇarūpiṇī. The devanagari script is not mere writing but a map of Devi's body.
usage_context: Chant during Sanskrit learning, Matrika (alphabet deity) meditation, or during Aksharamala (garland of letters) puja.

root_word: janma + mṛtyu + jarā + tapta + jana + viśrānti + dāyinī
meaning_en: She who gives rest and peace to those tormented by birth, death, and old age
meaning_hi: जन्म, मृत्यु और जरा (बुढ़ापा) से पीड़ित लोगों को विश्रान्ति (शान्ति) देने वाली
modern_context: जन्म, बुढ़ापा, मृत्यु, ये तीन unavoidable हैं। बुद्ध ने इन्हीं तीनों को देखकर गृह-त्याग किया। देवी इन तीनों से पीड़ित लोगों को शान्ति देती हैं। जब बच्चे का जन्म कठिन हो, जब बुढ़ापे में शरीर साथ न दे, जब मृत्यु का भय सताए, देवी विश्रान्ति (relief) देती हैं। Hospice care, geriatric care, neonatal care सब इसी सेवा का आधुनिक रूप।
modern_context_en: Birth, old age, death, these three are unavoidable. Buddha renounced the world upon seeing these three. Devi gives peace to those tormented by them. When childbirth is difficult, when old age fails the body, when death's fear haunts, Devi gives vishránti (relief). Hospice, geriatric, and neonatal care are modern forms of this seva.
nri_context: When ageing parents in India worry you from abroad, or when your own mortality surfaces in a health scare, Janmamṛtyujarātaptajanaviśrāntidāyinī offers the rest that no medicine alone can provide.
usage_context: Chant during life-transition anxiety, eldercare, hospice support, or when birth-death-ageing contemplation arises.

meaning_en: She who is proclaimed and celebrated by all the Upanishads
meaning_hi: सभी उपनिषदों द्वारा उद्घोषित और प्रशंसित

upaniṣad.en: Upanishads (the philosophical essence of the Vedas; 108+ texts)

modern_context: 108+ उपनिषद हैं: ईश, केन, कठ, प्रश्न, मुण्डक, माण्डूक्य, ऐतरेय, तैत्तिरीय, छान्दोग्य, बृहदारण्यक प्रमुख हैं। सब उपनिषद देवी की ही महिमा गाते हैं। जब कोई उपनिषद पढ़ें, वे देवी का proclamation पढ़ रहे हैं। Upanishad = उप (पास) + नि (नीचे) + षद् (बैठना) = गुरु के पास बैठकर सुनना।
modern_context_en: 108+ Upanishads exist: Isha, Kena, Katha, Prashna, Mundaka, Mandukya, Aitareya, Taittiriya, Chandogya, Brihadaranyaka are principal. All Upanishads proclaim Devi's glory. Reading any Upanishad is reading Devi's proclamation. Upanishad = Upa (near) + Ni (down) + Shad (sit) = sitting near the guru to listen.
nri_context: When studying Upanishads through online courses abroad, each text you read, from Isha to Mandukya, is Sarvopaniṣadudghuṣṭā's voice reaching you through humanity's oldest philosophical literature.
usage_context: Chant during Upanishad study, when seeking Vedantic truth, or during guru-student learning. Also during Upanishad Parayana.

meaning_en: She who is the essence of the kala (state) that transcends even peace itself
meaning_hi: शान्ति से भी परे की कला (अवस्था) का सार
modern_context: पाँच कलाएँ हैं: निवृत्ति, प्रतिष्ठा, विद्या, शान्ति, और शान्त्यतीत। शान्त्यतीत सबसे ऊँची कला है जो शान्ति से भी परे है। शान्ति में अभी 'शान्ति का अनुभव' है, शान्त्यतीत में अनुभवकर्ता ही विलीन। यह ऐसी अवस्था है जहाँ 'मैं शान्त हूँ' भी नहीं रहता, बस 'है'।
modern_context_en: Five kalas exist: Nivritti, Pratishtha, Vidya, Shanti, and Shantyatita. Shantyatita is the highest, beyond even peace. In peace there is still 'experience of peace'; in Shantyatita even the experiencer dissolves. This is the state where 'I am peaceful' also vanishes; only 'IS' remains.
nri_context: When meditation abroad reaches a state beyond even the sense of peacefulness, where the meditator and the peace merge into undifferentiated being, you touch Śāntyatītakalātmikā's transcendent essence.
usage_context: Chant during the deepest meditation, when even peace must be transcended, or during Pancha Kala study. The most advanced meditation name.

meaning_en: She who is profoundly deep, unfathomable as the ocean
meaning_hi: अत्यन्त गहन, समुद्र की तरह अथाह
modern_context: समुद्र की सतह पर लहरें हैं लेकिन गहराई में शान्ति। देवी गम्भीरा सतह पर लीला करती हैं लेकिन उनकी गहराई अथाह है। जब किसी व्यक्ति को 'गम्भीर' कहें, उसका अर्थ है उसमें depth है। Surface-level knowledge नहीं, deep understanding। Social media ने सबको shallow बनाया, गम्भीरता का अभ्यास ज़रूरी है।
modern_context_en: The ocean's surface has waves but its depths hold stillness. Devi Gambhira plays on the surface but her depth is unfathomable. When calling someone 'gambhir', it means they have depth. Not surface-level knowledge but deep understanding. Social media made everyone shallow; practising depth is essential.
nri_context: When the shallowness of social media culture abroad makes depth seem unfashionable, cultivating gambhīratā, deep thinking, deep feeling, deep living, becomes a countercultural act of devotion.
usage_context: Chant when seeking depth, during ocean meditation, or when cultivating profundity over superficiality.

meaning_en: She who dwells within the inner sky of consciousness, the Chidakasha
meaning_hi: चेतना के आन्तरिक आकाश (चिदाकाश) में निवास करने वाली
modern_context: दो आकाश हैं: बाहरी (भूताकाश, physical sky) और भीतरी (चिदाकाश, consciousness-sky)। देवी भीतरी आकाश में रहती हैं। जब आँखें बन्द करें और भीतर 'space' अनुभव करें, वह चिदाकाश है जहाँ देवी निवास करती हैं। बाहरी आकाश में तारे हैं, भीतरी आकाश में शान्ति। दोनों अनन्त हैं, दोनों में देवी हैं।
modern_context_en: Two skies exist: outer (Bhutakasha, physical sky) and inner (Chidakasha, consciousness-sky). Devi dwells in the inner sky. When closing eyes and experiencing inner 'space', that is Chidakasha where Devi resides. The outer sky has stars; the inner sky has peace. Both are infinite; Devi is in both.
nri_context: When the concrete ceiling of a cramped overseas apartment feels oppressive, closing your eyes reveals the Gaganāntaḥ, the infinite inner sky where Devi dwells, boundless and unrestricted by any walls.
usage_context: Chant during Chidakasha meditation, inner sky visualization, or when seeking spaciousness within. A fitting 820th name pointing always inward.

meaning_en: She who possesses noble divine pride, the dignified confidence of the supreme
meaning_hi: दिव्य गर्व (उदात्त आत्मविश्वास) से सम्पन्न, परम का गरिमामय आत्मविश्वास

garvitā.en: one possessing noble pride, dignified confidence (not arrogance but the rightful sovereignty of the supreme)
garvitā.hi: गर्विता, उदात्त गर्व वाली (अहंकार नहीं, बल्कि सर्वोच्च का न्यायसंगत स्वाभिमान)

modern_context: गर्व और अहंकार में अन्तर है। गर्व justified आत्मविश्वास है, अहंकार unjustified। जब ISRO Mars mission सफल करे तो गर्व। जब बिना merit के दूसरों को नीचा दिखाए तो अहंकार। देवी गर्विता हैं क्योंकि उनका गर्व justified है: वे सर्वोच्च हैं। जब बेटी first rank लाए तो माँ का गर्व गर्विता है।
modern_context_en: Pride and arrogance differ. Pride is justified confidence; arrogance is unjustified. When ISRO succeeds at a Mars mission, that is pride. When someone looks down on others without merit, that is arrogance. Devi is Garvita because her pride is justified: she IS supreme. When a daughter gets first rank, the mother's pride is Garvita.
nri_context: When you take justified pride in your achievements abroad, your career, your children's success, your community contributions, without looking down on others, that healthy self-respect is Garvitā's energy.
usage_context: Chant when cultivating healthy pride, during achievement celebrations, or when distinguishing pride from arrogance.

meaning_en: She who is passionately fond of sacred music and divine song
meaning_hi: पवित्र संगीत और दिव्य गान की अत्यन्त प्रेमी
modern_context: देवी संगीत की दीवानी हैं। सामवेद गायन का वेद है और देवी उसकी प्रेमी। जब कोई भजन गाए, कीर्तन करे, या शास्त्रीय संगीत बजाए, देवी उस ध्वनि की ओर आकर्षित होती हैं। जैसे सुगन्ध मधुमक्खी को खींचती है, वैसे ही संगीत देवी को खींचता है। Eternal Raga app का भजन section देवी को प्रसन्न करने का माध्यम है।
modern_context_en: Devi is passionately fond of music. Sama Veda is the Veda of song and Devi loves it. When someone sings bhajans, does kirtan, or plays classical music, Devi is drawn to that sound. Like fragrance attracts bees, music attracts Devi. Eternal Raga app's bhajan section is a medium to please Devi.
nri_context: When you play devotional music from the Eternal Raga app during your commute abroad, Gānalolupā Devi is drawn to your car, your headphones, your kitchen, wherever her beloved music plays.
usage_context: Chant before musical performances, during bhajan sessions, or when playing devotional music. Also during Nada Yoga and music therapy.

meaning_en: She who is free from all mental constructs and imagination
meaning_hi: सभी मानसिक कल्पनाओं और विकल्पों से मुक्त
modern_context: मन लगातार कल्पना करता है: 'अगर ऐसा हो तो?', 'शायद वैसा हो जाए।' देवी इन सब कल्पनाओं से मुक्त हैं। उन्हें imagine नहीं करना पड़ता, वे directly जानती हैं। Anxiety ज़्यादातर कल्पना (imagination of worst case) है, reality नहीं। जब मन शान्त हो और कल्पना रुक जाए, तभी सत्य दिखता है।
modern_context_en: The mind constantly imagines: 'what if this happens?', 'maybe that will occur.' Devi is free from all such imagining. She does not need to imagine; she directly knows. Anxiety is mostly imagination (of worst case), not reality. When the mind quiets and imagination stops, truth appears.
nri_context: When anxiety abroad creates worst-case scenarios that never materialize, recognizing that 90% of fears are kalpanā (imagination) and not reality brings you closer to Kalpanārahitā's clear seeing.
usage_context: Chant during anxiety management, when imagination runs wild, or during Nirvikalpa (imagination-free) meditation.

meaning_en: She who is the ultimate limit, the farthest boundary of all existence
meaning_hi: परम सीमा, सम्पूर्ण अस्तित्व की अन्तिम परिधि

kāṣṭhā.en: the ultimate limit, the farthest point, the final boundary; also: a unit of time (1/30 of a Kala)
kāṣṭhā.hi: परम सीमा, अन्तिम बिन्दु; समय की इकाई भी

modern_context: काष्ठा = ultimate limit। जब 'limit' को भी limit कहें, उसकी limit देवी हैं। Mathematics में infinity + 1 = infinity, लेकिन देवी उस infinity की भी काष्ठा (boundary) हैं। Vedic time measurement में काष्ठा सबसे छोटी इकाई है (3.2 microseconds), और देवी उस सूक्ष्मतम समय-इकाई भी हैं।
modern_context_en: Kashtha = ultimate limit. When 'limit' itself has a limit, that limit is Devi. In mathematics infinity + 1 = infinity, but Devi is the kashtha (boundary) of that infinity. In Vedic time measurement, Kashtha is the smallest unit (3.2 microseconds), and Devi is even that tiniest time-unit.
nri_context: When you push past every limit abroad, career ceiling broken, personal best surpassed, comfort zone expanded, the ultimate kāṣṭhā beyond all limits is Devi herself, the final frontier.
usage_context: Chant when pushing boundaries, during Vedic time-measurement study, or when contemplating infinity's edge.

meaning_en: She who is beyond all endings, the one in whom nothing ever terminates
meaning_hi: सभी अन्तों से परे, जिसमें कुछ भी कभी समाप्त नहीं होता
modern_context: अकान्ता = बिना अन्त की। सब कुछ ख़त्म होता है: पैसा, रिश्ते, शरीर, यश। लेकिन देवी कभी ख़त्म नहीं होतीं। 'The End' सिनेमा में आता है, जीवन में आता है, लेकिन देवी में कभी नहीं। जब किसी अध्याय का अन्त हो और दुःख हो, तो जानें: देवी अकान्ता हैं, कुछ ख़त्म नहीं, बस रूप बदलता है।
modern_context_en: Akanta = without end. Everything ends: money, relationships, body, fame. But Devi never ends. 'The End' appears in cinema and in life, but never in Devi. When a chapter ends and grief comes, know: Devi is Akanta; nothing truly ends, only forms change.
nri_context: When a chapter of immigrant life closes, a city left, a job ended, a friend departed, Akāntā whispers: nothing truly ends in Devi's universe, it only transforms into what comes next.
usage_context: Chant during endings, when grief over closure arises, or during Ananta (endless) meditation.

meaning_en: She who shares half the body of her beloved (Shiva), the Ardhanarishvara form
meaning_hi: अपने प्रिय (शिव) के आधे शरीर को साझा करने वाली, अर्धनारीश्वर रूप
modern_context: अर्धनारीश्वर भारतीय दर्शन की सबसे क्रान्तिकारी concept है: ईश्वर आधा पुरुष, आधा स्त्री। न पुरुष ऊपर, न स्त्री नीचे, बराबर। शिव का दायाँ अंग और शक्ति का बायाँ, दोनों मिलकर एक। LGBTQ+ community के लिए यह ancient validation है कि gender binary divine level पर भी exist नहीं करता।
modern_context_en: Ardhanarishvara is Indian philosophy's most revolutionary concept: God is half male, half female. Neither male superior nor female inferior, equal. Shiva's right half and Shakti's left, together one. For the LGBTQ+ community this is ancient validation that gender binary does not exist even at the divine level.
nri_context: When gender equality discussions abroad seek historical or philosophical grounding, Ardhanarishvara provides the most ancient and radical answer: God's own body is half feminine, half masculine, perfectly equal.
usage_context: Chant during Ardhanarishvara meditation, gender equality discussions, or during Shiva-Shakti unity contemplation.

meaning_en: She who is free from the chain of cause and effect
meaning_hi: कार्य-कारण (cause-effect) की श्रृंखला से मुक्त
modern_context: सब कुछ cause-effect chain में है: बीज → पेड़ → फल → बीज। Karma → Phala → Karma। लेकिन देवी इस chain से मुक्त हैं। उन्हें किसी कारण से काम नहीं करना पड़ता और उनके काम का कोई binding result नहीं। जब ध्यान में 'क्यों?' का प्रश्न विलीन हो, causality transcend हो, वही कार्यकारणनिर्मुक्ति।
modern_context_en: Everything is in a cause-effect chain: seed → tree → fruit → seed. Karma → Phala → Karma. But Devi is free from this chain. She does not act because of any cause and her actions have no binding result. When in meditation the question 'why?' dissolves and causality is transcended, that is Karyakarananirmukti.
nri_context: When the immigrant mindset of 'I do X to get Y' eventually gives way to doing things simply because they are right, without calculating returns, you taste the kāryakāraṇa freedom this name describes.
usage_context: Chant during Nishkama Karma practice, when transcending cause-effect thinking, or during advanced Vedanta study.

meaning_en: She who is joyfully immersed in the waves of divine love-play with Kameshvara
meaning_hi: कामेश्वर (शिव) के साथ दिव्य प्रेम-क्रीड़ा की तरंगों में आनन्दपूर्वक डूबी हुई

taraṅgitā.en: wave-like, rippling with, immersed in waves

modern_context: शिव-शक्ति की प्रेम-क्रीड़ा ब्रह्माण्ड को गतिमान रखती है। Electron और proton का attraction, चन्द्रमा और पृथ्वी का gravitational dance, वसन्त में फूलों का खिलना, सब दिव्य कामकेलि है। जब पति-पत्नी हँसते-खेलते जीवन बिताएँ, वह शिव-शक्ति केलि का मानवीय रूप है।
modern_context_en: Shiva-Shakti's love-play keeps the cosmos moving. Electron-proton attraction, moon-earth gravitational dance, flowers blooming in spring, all are divine Kamakeli. When husband and wife spend life laughing and playing together, that is the human form of Shiva-Shakti Keli.
nri_context: When a couple abroad finds joy not just in achievements but in everyday playfulness, cooking together, dancing in the kitchen, laughing at nothing, those waves of love are Kāmakelit araṅgitā's cosmic ripples.
usage_context: Chant during Shiva-Shakti love meditation, when celebrating marital playfulness, or during Saundarya Lahari study.

meaning_en: She who wears dazzling golden ear ornaments that illuminate her face
meaning_hi: चमचमाते सोने के कान के आभूषण (ताटंक) से शोभित
modern_context: ताटंक (ear ornaments) सोलह शृंगार में शामिल हैं। कान में आभूषण पहनना केवल सजावट नहीं, acupressure point activation भी है। कान की लोब (earlobe) में piercing से brain की कुछ नसें stimulate होती हैं। Ayurveda कहता है कान में सोना पहनने से eye-sight improve होती है।
modern_context_en: Tatanka (ear ornaments) are part of sixteen adornments. Wearing ear ornaments is not just decoration but acupressure point activation. Earlobe piercing stimulates certain brain nerves. Ayurveda says wearing gold in ears improves eyesight.
nri_context: When you wear traditional jhumkas or tatankas to a formal event abroad, those golden ornaments carry both Kanatkanakatāṭaṅkā's blessing and the Ayurvedic wisdom of acupressure activation.
usage_context: Chant during ear-piercing ceremonies, Solah Shringar, or when wearing traditional ear ornaments.

meaning_en: She who assumes divine forms playfully for the joy of cosmic sport
meaning_hi: ब्रह्माण्डीय लीला के आनन्द के लिए खेल-खेल में दिव्य रूप धारण करने वाली
modern_context: देवी को form लेने की 'ज़रूरत' नहीं, वे 'खेल' में form लेती हैं। जैसे actor को role play करने की ज़रूरत नहीं होती लेकिन आनन्द के लिए करता है, वैसे ही देवी दुर्गा, लक्ष्मी, सरस्वती, काली, सब रूप लीला से लेती हैं। हर अवतार एक role है, और देवी सबसे बड़ी actor।
modern_context_en: Devi does not 'need' to take form; she takes form as 'play'. Like an actor does not need to play roles but does so for joy, Devi assumes Durga, Lakshmi, Saraswati, Kali forms playfully. Every avatar is a role, and Devi is the greatest actor.
nri_context: When you play different roles abroad, professional at work, parent at home, Indian at the temple, global citizen in the neighbourhood, each role is a līlā-vigraha that Devi herself models through her own cosmic role-playing.
usage_context: Chant when contemplating Devi's many forms, during Avatar study, or when life's roles feel overwhelming. Also during Leela meditation.

meaning_en: She who is unborn, never having come into existence through any process
meaning_hi: अजन्मा, किसी प्रक्रिया से अस्तित्व में नहीं आई

ajā.en: the unborn one (feminine of Aja); she who was never created, always existing
ajā.hi: अजा, अजन्मा; जो कभी सृजित नहीं हुई, सदा विद्यमान

modern_context: गीता में कृष्ण कहते हैं 'अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा', मैं अजन्मा और अविनाशी होते हुए भी प्रकट होता हूँ। देवी अजा हैं: न जन्मीं, न मरेंगी। जो जन्मा नहीं वह मर नहीं सकता। आपकी आत्मा भी अजा है। शरीर जन्मा, आत्मा नहीं। इसलिए मृत्यु का भय व्यर्थ है।
modern_context_en: In Gita Krishna says 'Ajo pi san avyayatma', though unborn and imperishable I still manifest. Devi is Aja: never born, never dying. What is never born cannot die. Your soul too is Aja. The body was born; the soul was not. Therefore fear of death is futile.
nri_context: When mortality anxiety surfaces abroad, far from family who would perform last rites, this name grounds you: the essential you is Ajā, unborn and undying, immune to the body's fate.
usage_context: Chant during Gita Chapter 2 study, death-anxiety management, or during immortal-soul meditation.

meaning_en: She who is completely free from decay, decline, and diminution
meaning_hi: क्षय (ह्रास, गिरावट, कमी) से पूर्णतः मुक्त
modern_context: सब कुछ क्षय (decay) होता है: शरीर बूढ़ा होता है, इमारतें गिरती हैं, suns भी die करते हैं (supernova)। लेकिन देवी कभी क्षय नहीं होतीं। Second Law of Thermodynamics (entropy increases) सब पर लागू है, लेकिन देवी पर नहीं। Anti-ageing cream ख़रीदने से बेहतर, क्षय-मुक्त देवी का ध्यान करें।
modern_context_en: Everything decays: bodies age, buildings crumble, suns die (supernova). But Devi never decays. The Second Law of Thermodynamics (entropy increases) applies to all except Devi. Instead of buying anti-ageing cream, meditate on the decay-free Devi.
nri_context: When ageing abroad feels isolating and the body's decline seems relentless, this name offers existential comfort: the Devi within you, your consciousness, is Kṣayavinirmuktā, immune to every form of decay.
usage_context: Chant during ageing contemplation, when entropy seems overwhelming, or during Amrita (immortality) meditation.

meaning_en: She who is enchantingly innocent, possessing the freshness of eternal youth
meaning_hi: मन्त्रमुग्ध करने वाली मासूमियत और शाश्वत यौवन की ताज़गी वाली

mugdhā.en: enchantingly innocent, fresh, naively charming, the young heroine in love
mugdhā.hi: मुग्धा, मोहक मासूमियत, ताज़ी, प्रेम में नई नायिका

modern_context: काव्यशास्त्र में मुग्धा नायिका वह है जो प्रेम में नई हो: शर्मीली, मासूम, और ताज़ी। देवी सर्वज्ञ होते हुए भी मुग्धा हैं, अर्थात् उनमें ताज़गी कभी नहीं मरती। जब 50 साल बाद भी पति-पत्नी में वही flutter हो जो पहली मुलाक़ात में था, वह मुग्धा भाव है। Innocence ageless है।
modern_context_en: In literary theory, Mugdha Nayika is the heroine new to love: shy, innocent, fresh. Devi though omniscient is also Mugdha, meaning her freshness never dies. When after 50 years a couple still feels the flutter of their first meeting, that is Mugdha Bhava. Innocence is ageless.
nri_context: When you experience something abroad with childlike wonder, first snowfall, first cherry blossom, first Northern Lights, that mugdhā innocence is Devi looking through your eyes with eternal freshness.
usage_context: Chant when cultivating wonder, during romantic freshness meditation, or when innocence needs protection.

meaning_en: She who is swiftly pleased and grants grace with lightning speed
meaning_hi: शीघ्र प्रसन्न होने वाली, बिजली की गति से कृपा करने वाली

prasādinī.hi: प्रसन्न होने वाली, कृपा करने वाली

modern_context: देवी जल्दी प्रसन्न होती हैं। Sadyahprasadini (493) तत्काल कृपा देती हैं और क्षिप्रप्रसादिनी शीघ्र प्रसन्न होती हैं। दोनों similar लेकिन भिन्न: एक grace की speed, दूसरी pleasure की speed। जब एक फूल चढ़ाएँ और तुरन्त peace अनुभव हो, वह क्षिप्र-प्रसाद। कठिन rituals ज़रूरी नहीं, सच्चा भाव ज़रूरी।
modern_context_en: Devi is quickly pleased. Sadyahprasadini (493) gives instant grace; Kshipraprasadini is swiftly pleased. Both similar but different: one is speed of grace, other is speed of being pleased. When offering one flower and instantly feeling peace, that is Kshipra-Prasada. Elaborate rituals are not necessary; sincere feeling is.
nri_context: When a hurried prayer abroad before rushing to work, just one heartfelt 'Ma', brings immediate inner calm, Kṣipraprasādinī was pleased in that split second of sincerity.
usage_context: Chant when seeking quick blessings, during emergency prayers, or when reassuring that Devi is easily pleased.

meaning_en: She who is properly worshipped by those who turn their awareness inward
meaning_hi: जो अपनी चेतना को अन्तर्मुख (भीतर) करते हैं उनके द्वारा भली-भाँति आराधित
modern_context: बाहरी पूजा (मन्दिर, मूर्ति, धूप-दीप) शुरुआत है, लेकिन असली पूजा अन्तर्मुखी है। जब आँखें बन्द करें, इन्द्रियाँ भीतर मोड़ें, और चेतना को अन्दर direct करें, वही सबसे शक्तिशाली आराधना। Pratyahara (इन्द्रिय-निवर्तन) yoga का पाँचवाँ अंग है और अन्तर्मुख होने की विधि। बाहर देखना सरल, भीतर देखना साधना।
modern_context_en: External puja (temple, murti, incense) is the beginning but real worship is inward-turned. When closing eyes, withdrawing senses inward, and directing awareness within, that is the most powerful worship. Pratyahara (sense-withdrawal) is yoga's fifth limb and the method of turning inward. Looking outside is easy; looking inside is sadhana.
nri_context: When elaborate temple worship is unavailable abroad but you close your eyes during a lunch break and turn inward for 5 minutes, that antarmukha worship is more pleasing to Devi than any outward ritual done mechanically.
usage_context: Chant during internal worship, Pratyahara practice, or when choosing inner meditation over external ritual. One of the most important sadhana-guidance names.

meaning_en: She who is extremely difficult to attain for those whose awareness faces only outward
meaning_hi: बहिर्मुख (बाहर देखने वालों) के लिए अत्यन्त दुर्लभ
modern_context: यह नाम पिछले (835) का complement है: भीतर देखो तो देवी मिलती हैं, बाहर देखो तो दुर्लभ। Social media, shopping, entertainment सब बाहर खींचते हैं। जितना बाहर भागें, देवी उतनी दूर। जितना भीतर जाएँ, देवी उतनी पास। 'God is within' सभी mystics का common teaching है, और यह नाम इसकी scriptural confirmation।
modern_context_en: This name complements the previous (835): look within and Devi is found; look outward and she is rare. Social media, shopping, entertainment all pull outward. The more you run outside, the farther Devi. The more you go within, the closer Devi. 'God is within' is every mystic's common teaching, and this name is its scriptural confirmation.
nri_context: When the outward-facing culture abroad, consumerism, social media, constant distraction, makes Devi seem impossibly far, this name explains precisely why: she is found within, never without.
usage_context: Chant during digital detox, when external seeking exhausts, or when redirecting from outer to inner. Pairs perfectly with name 835.

meaning_en: She who is the Trayi, the triple Veda (Rig, Yajur, Sama)
meaning_hi: त्रयी, तीन वेदों (ऋग्, यजुर्, साम) का स्वरूप

trayī.en: the Triple (the three Vedas: Rig, Yajur, Sama; Atharva was added later)
trayī.hi: त्रयी (तीन वेद: ऋग्, यजुर्, साम; अथर्व बाद में जुड़ा)

modern_context: मूल रूप से तीन वेद (ऋग्, यजुर्, साम) 'त्रयी' कहलाते थे। ऋग्वेद = ज्ञान के मन्त्र, यजुर्वेद = यज्ञ की विधि, सामवेद = गायन। तीनों मिलकर पूर्ण ज्ञान हैं: जानो (ऋग्), करो (यजुर्), गाओ (साम)। Knowledge + Practice + Joy = Complete Wisdom। देवी त्रयी तीनों का संयुक्त रूप हैं।
modern_context_en: Originally three Vedas (Rig, Yajur, Sama) were called 'Trayi'. Rig Veda = knowledge mantras, Yajur Veda = ritual procedure, Sama Veda = singing. Together they form complete knowledge: Know (Rig), Do (Yajur), Sing (Sama). Knowledge + Practice + Joy = Complete Wisdom. Devi Trayi is their combined form.
nri_context: When your spiritual practice abroad integrates study (Rig), ritual action (Yajur), and devotional singing (Sama), all three together, you honour the complete Trayī that Devi embodies.
usage_context: Chant during Veda study, when integrating knowledge-action-joy, or during Veda Parayana.

meaning_en: She who is the abode of the three worldly goals (Dharma, Artha, Kama)
meaning_hi: तीन लौकिक लक्ष्यों (धर्म, अर्थ, काम) का निवास

varga.en: group, category (here: Dharma, Artha, Kama, the three worldly goals excluding Moksha)
varga.hi: वर्ग (धर्म, अर्थ, काम, मोक्ष-रहित तीन लौकिक लक्ष्य)

modern_context: चार पुरुषार्थ में से तीन (धर्म, अर्थ, काम) लौकिक हैं: नैतिक जीना, धन कमाना, इच्छाएँ पूरी करना। चौथा (मोक्ष) लोकोत्तर है। देवी तीनों लौकिक लक्ष्यों का निवास हैं, अर्थात् सांसारिक सफलता भी देवी से ही आती है। MBA करना, business चलाना, family बसाना, सब देवी की कृपा।
modern_context_en: Of four Purusharthas, three (Dharma, Artha, Kama) are worldly: ethical living, wealth creation, desire fulfilment. The fourth (Moksha) is transcendental. Devi is the abode of the three worldly goals, meaning worldly success also comes from Devi. Doing an MBA, running a business, building a family, all are Devi's grace.
nri_context: When building material success abroad, ethical career, financial stability, and family happiness, these three trivarga goals are not distractions from spirituality but Devi's own domain.
usage_context: Chant during worldly goal-setting, career planning, or when integrating material and spiritual aspirations.

meaning_en: She who abides in three places simultaneously: Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva's realms
meaning_hi: तीन स्थानों पर एक साथ विराजमान: ब्रह्मा, विष्णु और शिव के लोक
modern_context: देवी तीन लोकों (स्वर्ग, पृथ्वी, पाताल) में, तीन देवताओं (ब्रह्मा, विष्णु, शिव) में, और तीन अवस्थाओं (जाग्रत, स्वप्न, सुषुप्ति) में एक साथ हैं। Quantum physics में एक particle तीन जगह एक साथ हो सकता है (superposition), देवी cosmic superposition में हैं।
modern_context_en: Devi is simultaneously in three worlds (heaven, earth, underworld), three gods (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva), and three states (waking, dreaming, deep sleep). In quantum physics a particle can be in three places simultaneously (superposition); Devi is in cosmic superposition.
nri_context: When you feel torn between three worlds abroad, work, home, and India, this name reassures: Devi is simultaneously in all three, and so can your awareness be. You do not have to choose one.
usage_context: Chant during triloka meditation, when managing multiple domains, or during quantum-spirituality discussions.

meaning_en: She who wears the three cities as a garland, the sovereign of all realms
meaning_hi: तीन पुरों (नगरों) को माला की तरह धारण करने वाली

tripura.en: three cities (the three states, three bodies, three worlds)
tripura.hi: तीन पुर (तीन अवस्थाएँ, तीन शरीर, तीन लोक)

mālinī.en: one who wears a garland, one who strings together

modern_context: त्रिपुरमालिनी 10वीं नित्या देवी और एक महत्त्वपूर्ण तन्त्र ग्रन्थ ('तन्त्रराज तन्त्र') की अधिष्ठात्री हैं। 'मालिनी' शब्द से 'मालिनी विजय तन्त्र' नामक ग्रन्थ भी जुड़ा है। तीन पुरों (gold, silver, iron cities जो बन्धन के प्रतीक) को माला बनाकर पहनना = बन्धनों को आभूषण बनाना। Challenges को ornaments बनाएँ।
modern_context_en: Tripuramalini is the 10th Nitya Devi and presiding deity of an important tantra text ('Tantraraja Tantra'). The word 'Malini' connects to 'Malini Vijaya Tantra'. Wearing three cities (gold, silver, iron cities symbolizing bondage) as a garland = turning bondage into ornament. Make challenges into ornaments.
nri_context: When the three challenges of immigrant life, financial pressure, cultural displacement, identity crisis, are worn not as chains but as ornaments of experience, you embody Tripuramālinī's transformative power.
usage_context: Chant during Nitya Devi worship, Tantra study, or when transforming challenges into strengths.

meaning_en: She who is completely free from all disease and suffering
meaning_hi: सभी रोग और पीड़ा से पूर्णतः मुक्त
modern_context: निरामय = बिना रोग। देवी स्वयं रोग-मुक्त हैं और भक्तों को भी रोग-मुक्त करती हैं। Dhanvantari (Ayurveda के देवता) भी देवी की शक्ति से काम करते हैं। WHO का लक्ष्य 'Health for All' है, देवी का लक्ष्य 'Niramayta for All'। जब बीमार हों तो Doctor के पास जाएँ और देवी से भी प्रार्थना करें, दोनों ज़रूरी।
modern_context_en: Niramaya = disease-free. Devi herself is disease-free and makes devotees disease-free too. Dhanvantari (Ayurveda's deity) also works through Devi's power. WHO's goal is 'Health for All'; Devi's goal is 'Niramayta for All'. When sick, visit a doctor AND pray to Devi; both are needed.
nri_context: When navigating healthcare abroad, insurance complexities, unfamiliar medical systems, combining medical treatment with prayer to Nirāmayā bridges the clinical and the spiritual in the healing journey.
usage_context: Chant during illness, health-seeking, or during Dhanvantari/Ayurveda puja. Also during World Health Day.

meaning_en: She who needs no support, the self-sustaining absolute
meaning_hi: किसी सहारे की आवश्यकता न रखने वाली, स्वयं-पोषित परम तत्त्व
modern_context: निरालम्बा = बिना सहारे। सब कुछ किसी पर निर्भर है: पेड़ ज़मीन पर, चन्द्रमा पृथ्वी पर, economy consumers पर। लेकिन देवी किसी पर निर्भर नहीं, सब उन पर निर्भर हैं। Self-sustaining = Niralamba। जब आप इतने self-reliant हों कि किसी की need न हो (लेकिन want हो), वह स्वस्थ निरालम्बता है।
modern_context_en: Niralamba = without support. Everything depends on something: trees on ground, moon on earth, economy on consumers. But Devi depends on nothing; everything depends on her. Self-sustaining = Niralamba. When you are self-reliant enough to need nobody (but want their company), that is healthy Niralambata.
nri_context: When the initial dependency of immigration gives way to self-sufficiency abroad, no longer needing hand-holding but choosing community, that healthy independence echoes Nirālambā's self-sustaining nature.
usage_context: Chant during self-reliance cultivation, when dependency needs releasing, or during Niralamba Upanishad study.

meaning_en: She who delights in her own Self, finding all joy within
meaning_hi: अपने स्वयं के आत्मा में आनन्दित, सभी सुख भीतर पाने वाली

ārāmā.en: one who delights, finds joy (also: garden of bliss)

modern_context: स्वात्माराम = स्वयं में आनन्द पाने वाली। Hatha Yoga Pradipika के लेखक 'स्वात्माराम' इसी शब्द से नामित हैं। जब बाहरी entertainment बन्द हो (phone, TV, friends सब ग़ायब) और फिर भी आनन्द हो, वह स्वात्माराम। Boredom का विपरीत self-entertainment नहीं, self-joy (आत्म-आनन्द) है।
modern_context_en: Svatmarama = one who delights in the Self. Hatha Yoga Pradipika's author 'Svatmarama' is named from this word. When external entertainment stops (phone, TV, friends gone) yet joy remains, that is Svatmarama. Boredom's opposite is not self-entertainment but self-joy (atma-ananda).
nri_context: When a power outage abroad kills WiFi, phone dies, and you sit in silence and discover unexpected contentment within, that self-sufficient joy is Svātmārāmā's natural state revealing itself.
usage_context: Chant during solitude practice, Hatha Yoga Pradipika study, or when inner joy surpasses outer entertainment.

meaning_en: She from whom flows the immortal nectar (Amrita) of bliss
meaning_hi: जिनसे आनन्द का अमृत (सुधा) बहता है
modern_context: सुधा = अमृत। समुद्र मन्थन में अमृत निकला जिसे देवताओं ने पिया और अमर हुए। देवी से भी अमृत बहता है। योग में जब कुण्डलिनी सहस्रार पहुँचती है तो 'अमृत' (bliss-nectar) टपकता है जिसे कण्ठ में अनुभव किया जाता है। Khechari Mudra में जीभ को तालू के पीछे लगाकर इस अमृत को taste किया जाता है।
modern_context_en: Sudha = Amrita (nectar). During Samudra Manthana, Amrita emerged and gods drank it to become immortal. Amrita also flows from Devi. In yoga when Kundalini reaches Sahasrara, 'Amrita' (bliss-nectar) drips, experienced at the throat. In Khechari Mudra, the tongue touches the palate to taste this nectar.
nri_context: When a deep meditation session abroad produces an inexplicable sweetness in the mouth or a nectar-like bliss dripping through the body, Sudhāsṛtiḥ's amrita flow is making itself known.
usage_context: Chant during Kundalini yoga, Khechari Mudra practice, or when experiencing nectar-like bliss in meditation.

meaning_en: She who is expert at rescuing those drowning in the mud of worldly existence
meaning_hi: संसार के कीचड़ में डूबे लोगों को निकालने में निपुण
modern_context: संसार कीचड़ (mud) है और हम उसमें डूबे हैं। देवी expert rescue worker हैं। NDRF जैसे बाढ़ में डूबे लोगों को बचाती है, देवी संसार-बाढ़ से बचाती हैं। जब addiction, debt, toxic relationship, या depression के कीचड़ में फँसें और बाहर निकलना असम्भव लगे, तो देवी expert rescuer हैं।
modern_context_en: Samsara is mud and we are sunk in it. Devi is an expert rescue worker. Like NDRF saves those drowning in floods, Devi rescues from the flood of samsara. When stuck in the mud of addiction, debt, toxic relationships, or depression with no way out, Devi is the expert rescuer.
nri_context: When you feel stuck abroad in a situation you cannot escape, visa complications, financial debt, toxic work environment, with no visible exit, Saṃsārapaṅka-rescue-expert Devi is the divine lifeline.
usage_context: Chant when feeling stuck, during crisis intervention, or when rescuing others from despair. The most practically reassuring name.

meaning_en: She who loves sacred fire rituals (yajnas) and all acts of selfless offering
meaning_hi: यज्ञ (पवित्र अग्नि अनुष्ठान) और निःस्वार्थ अर्पण से प्रसन्न

yajña.en: sacred fire ritual, sacrifice, any selfless offering

modern_context: गीता में कृष्ण कहते हैं 'यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धनः', यज्ञ के लिए किए गए कर्म के अलावा सब कर्म बन्धन है। यज्ञ = selfless offering। जब doctor बिना fees लिए ग़रीब का इलाज करे, जब teacher extra class मुफ्त में ले, जब आप anonymous donation करें, सब यज्ञ। हवन कुण्ड में आहुति सबसे पुराना यज्ञ है।
modern_context_en: In Gita Krishna says 'Yajnarthat Karmano Anyatra Loko Ayam Karmabandhana', work done as yajna does not bind; all other work does. Yajna = selfless offering. When a doctor treats the poor for free, when a teacher gives extra classes without charge, when you donate anonymously, all are yajna. Havan is the most ancient yajna.
nri_context: When your work abroad transforms from 'earning a living' to 'offering my skills to the world', that shift from self-interest to yajña spirit is what Yajñapriyā loves and blesses.
usage_context: Chant during havan, selfless service, or when transforming work into offering. Also during Gita Chapter 3 study.

meaning_en: She who is the ultimate performer of all cosmic yajnas
meaning_hi: सभी ब्रह्माण्डीय यज्ञों की परम कर्ता
