modern_context_en: Seeing Devi's form instantly generates auspicious feelings. When darshan at a temple immediately brings peace, removes negativity, and purifies the mind, that is Bhadramurti's effect. Some people's mere presence is calming; they are Bhadramurtis. The company of 'good people' is the greatest worship.
nri_context: When a Devi murti at your diaspora temple or home altar radiates such peaceful energy that guests remark 'your puja room feels so calm', that is Bhadramūrtiḥ's auspiciousness emanating through the form.
usage_context: Chant during murti darshan, when seeking auspicious company, or during Satsang.

meaning_en: She who bestows good fortune and marital bliss upon her devotees
meaning_hi: भक्तों को सौभाग्य (अच्छा भाग्य, दाम्पत्य सुख) प्रदान करने वाली

saubhāgya.en: good fortune, marital bliss, all-round auspiciousness

modern_context: सौभाग्य = सु + भाग्य = अच्छा भाग्य। विशेष रूप से दाम्पत्य सौभाग्य (marital bliss)। शुक्रवार का व्रत, महालक्ष्मी पूजा, और सौभाग्य-सूत्र (mangalsutra) सब सौभाग्य से जुड़े। जब married life सुखी हो, बच्चे स्वस्थ हों, और घर में शान्ति हो, वह सौभाग्य। Single लोगों के लिए सौभाग्य = सर्वांगीण भाग्य।
modern_context_en: Saubhagya = good fortune, especially marital bliss. Friday fasts, Mahalakshmi puja, and Saubhagya-sutra (mangalsutra) all connect to Saubhagya. When married life is happy, children healthy, home peaceful, that is Saubhagya. For single people, Saubhagya = all-round fortune.
nri_context: When a happy marriage abroad, nurtured through daily kindness, mutual respect, and shared devotion, becomes the foundation of a thriving family, that is Bhaktasaubhāgyadāyinī's direct blessing.
usage_context: Chant during Saubhagya puja, wedding ceremonies, or when seeking marital/overall fortune.

meaning_en: She who loves devotion above all else, who is won by sincere bhakti alone
meaning_hi: भक्ति (समर्पण) से सर्वोपरि प्रेम करने वाली, केवल सच्ची भक्ति से प्रसन्न होने वाली
modern_context: देवी को ज्ञान, तन्त्र, योग सब से प्रसन्न किया जा सकता है, लेकिन सबसे ज़्यादा प्रसन्न भक्ति (devotion) से होती हैं। Mirabai जो केवल प्रेम जानती थीं, Ramakrishna जो बच्चे की तरह रोकर माँ को बुलाते थे, Andal जो गीत गाकर भगवान से विवाह कर लिया, सब भक्तिप्रिया को प्रसन्न करने वाले। Technique नहीं, feeling matters।
modern_context_en: Devi can be pleased through knowledge, tantra, and yoga, but is most pleased by bhakti (devotion). Mirabai who knew only love, Ramakrishna who cried like a child calling Mother, Andal who sang and married the Lord, all pleased Bhaktipriya. Technique does not matter; feeling does.
nri_context: When your worship abroad is imperfect in form but overflowing in feeling, when tears come during an off-key bhajan, when broken Sanskrit carries a full heart, Bhaktipriyā values that bhakti above any flawless ritual.
usage_context: Chant when devotion overflows, during Bhakti Yoga, or when sincerity surpasses technique.

meaning_en: She who is attainable solely through devotion
meaning_hi: केवल भक्ति के माध्यम से प्राप्त होने योग्य
modern_context: पिछला नाम (915) कहता है देवी भक्ति से प्रसन्न, यह कहता है देवी भक्ति से ही प्राप्य। अर्थात् PhD, wealth, power, fame कुछ भी देवी तक नहीं पहुँचा सकता, केवल भक्ति। गीता कहती है 'भक्त्या मामभिजानाति', मुझे भक्ति से ही जाना जा सकता है। सबसे सरल और सबसे शक्तिशाली मार्ग: भक्ति।
modern_context_en: Previous name (915) says Devi loves bhakti; this says Devi is attainable ONLY through bhakti. PhD, wealth, power, fame cannot reach Devi; only bhakti can. Gita says 'Bhaktya Mam Abhijanati', I am known only through devotion. The simplest and most powerful path: bhakti.
nri_context: When all worldly achievements abroad still leave a God-shaped hole, this name clarifies: Devi is not reached through success metrics but through the bhakti that even a child can offer.
usage_context: Chant during Bhakti Yoga, Gita Chapter 11/18 study, or when intellectual approaches to God fail and only love remains.

meaning_en: She who is conquered and controlled by the devotion of her bhaktas
meaning_hi: भक्तों की भक्ति से वश (नियन्त्रित, जीती हुई)

vaśyā.en: conquered by, under the control of, won over by

modern_context: सबसे revolutionary concept: ब्रह्माण्ड की स्वामिनी भक्त की भक्ति से 'वश' (controlled) हो जाती हैं! जैसे माँ बच्चे के प्रेम से बँध जाती है और बच्चा जो माँगे वही करती है, देवी भी भक्त की भक्ति से बँधती हैं। Yashoda ने कृष्ण को रस्सी से बाँधा, भक्ति की रस्सी से भगवान बँधते हैं। 915-916-917 bhakti trilogy complete।
modern_context_en: The most revolutionary concept: the cosmos's sovereign is 'controlled' by a devotee's bhakti! Like a mother is bound by a child's love and does whatever the child wants, Devi is bound by bhakti. Yashoda tied Krishna with a rope; God is bound by the rope of devotion. The 915-916-917 bhakti trilogy completes.
nri_context: When your sincere devotion abroad seems to 'move' Devi, when prayers are answered in ways that feel like a mother yielding to her child's plea, you have experienced Bhaktivaśyā's beautiful surrender to your love.
usage_context: Chant when devotion overwhelms, during bhakti celebrations, or when Devi seems to respond to prayer. Completes the bhakti trilogy.

meaning_en: She who completely removes all fear from the hearts of her devotees
meaning_hi: भक्तों के हृदय से सम्पूर्ण भय को दूर करने वाली
modern_context: भय मनुष्य का सबसे बड़ा शत्रु है। मृत्यु का भय, गरीबी का भय, अकेलेपन का भय, असफलता का भय। देवी सब भय दूर करती हैं। Abhaya Mudra (अभय मुद्रा, 'मत डरो' का हाथ-संकेत) देवी और सभी देवताओं की सबसे प्रसिद्ध मुद्रा है। जब भय आए, देवी का हाथ देखें जो कह रहा है 'मत डरो, मैं हूँ।'
modern_context_en: Fear is humanity's greatest enemy. Fear of death, poverty, loneliness, failure. Devi removes all fears. Abhaya Mudra ('fear not' hand gesture) is the most famous mudra of Devi and all deities. When fear comes, see Devi's hand saying 'do not fear, I am here.'
nri_context: When fear of deportation, fear of failure, fear of the unknown abroad paralyzes you, Bhayāpahā's Abhaya Mudra is raised eternally, saying 'do not be afraid, I am with you always.'
usage_context: Chant during fear, anxiety attacks, or when courage is needed. Also during Abhaya Mudra meditation.

meaning_en: She who belongs to Shambhu (Shiva), embodying the Shambhavi state of consciousness
meaning_hi: शम्भु (शिव) की, शाम्भवी चेतना-अवस्था का स्वरूप

śāmbhavī.en: belonging to Shambhu (Shiva); the Shambhavi state (a high meditative state); Shambhavi Mudra (gazing at the third eye)
śāmbhavī.hi: शम्भु की; शाम्भवी अवस्था (उच्च ध्यान-अवस्था); शाम्भवी मुद्रा

modern_context: शाम्भवी मुद्रा = आँखें आधी खुली, दृष्टि भ्रूमध्य (third eye) पर, बाहर देखते हुए भीतर देखना। Isha Foundation की 'Inner Engineering' program में Shambhavi Mahamudra सिखाई जाती है जो millions practise करते हैं। देवी शाम्भवी उस अवस्था हैं जहाँ बाहर और भीतर एक हो जाएँ। Open-eyed meditation सबसे advanced है।
modern_context_en: Shambhavi Mudra = eyes half-open, gaze at third eye, looking outward while seeing inward. Isha Foundation's 'Inner Engineering' teaches Shambhavi Mahamudra practised by millions. Devi Shambhavi is the state where outer and inner become one. Open-eyed meditation is the most advanced.
nri_context: When you can maintain meditative awareness abroad while working, walking, and talking, seeing the world while remaining centred within, you practise the Śāmbhavī state in daily life.
usage_context: Chant during Shambhavi Mudra practice, open-eyed meditation, or during Shiva-Shakti consciousness study.

meaning_en: She who is worshipped by Sharada (Saraswati), the goddess of learning
meaning_hi: शारदा (सरस्वती, विद्या की देवी) द्वारा आराधित

śāradā.en: Sharada (Saraswati; the autumn goddess of wisdom; also: Sharada Peeth in Kashmir)
śāradā.hi: शारदा (सरस्वती; शरद ऋतु की ज्ञान-देवी; कश्मीर का शारदा पीठ भी)

modern_context: सरस्वती (शारदा) स्वयं विद्या की देवी हैं, लेकिन वे भी ललिता की आराधना करती हैं। अर्थात् ज्ञान भी भक्ति का सेवक है। Sringeri Sharada Peetham (कर्नाटक) और Sharada Peeth (कश्मीर, अब PoK में) दोनों शारदा देवी के प्रमुख स्थान हैं। शरद पूर्णिमा (Sharad Purnima) शारदा-ललिता पूजा का विशेष दिन।
modern_context_en: Saraswati (Sharada) is the goddess of learning herself, yet she worships Lalita. Meaning even knowledge serves devotion. Sringeri Sharada Peetham (Karnataka) and Sharada Peeth (Kashmir, now in PoK) are Sharada Devi's primary seats. Sharad Purnima is a special day for Sharada-Lalita worship.
nri_context: When intellectual achievements abroad reach their pinnacle and yet bow before something deeper, the recognition that even Saraswati worships Lalita reframes knowledge as a means, not an end.
usage_context: Chant during Sharad Purnima, Sringeri pilgrimage, or during Saraswati-Lalita relationship contemplation.

meaning_en: She who is Sharvani, the consort of Sharva (Shiva as the archer/destroyer)
meaning_hi: शर्वाणी, शर्व (शिव, धनुर्धर/संहारक) की पत्नी

śarvāṇī.en: consort of Sharva (Shiva as the archer; one who destroys suffering with his arrows; one of Shiva's 8 forms: Ashtamurti)
śarvāṇī.hi: शर्व की पत्नी (शिव धनुर्धर रूप; दुःख का बाणों से नाश करने वाले; अष्टमूर्ति में एक)

modern_context: शर्व शिव का वह रूप है जो बाणों (arrows) से दुःख का नाश करता है। Arjuna को Pashupatastra शर्व (शिव) से ही मिला। देवी शर्वाणी उस archer-Shiva की शक्ति हैं। जब कोई problem 'targeted solution' चाहे (arrow = precise), शर्वाणी से प्रार्थना करें। Scatter-gun approach नहीं, focused strike।
modern_context_en: Sharva is Shiva's archer form who destroys suffering with arrows. Arjuna received Pashupatastra from Sharva. Devi Sharvani is that archer-Shiva's Shakti. When a problem needs a 'targeted solution' (arrow = precise), pray to Sharvani. Not scatter-gun approach but focused strike.
nri_context: When a specific, targeted problem abroad needs a precise solution rather than a broad approach, Śarvāṇī's arrow-like energy provides the focused clarity to hit exactly the right mark.
usage_context: Chant when precision is needed, during targeted problem-solving, or during Shiva's Ashtamurti study.

meaning_en: She who bestows supreme happiness, comfort, and blissful shelter
meaning_hi: परम सुख, आराम और आनन्दमय आश्रय देने वाली
modern_context: शर्मा (Sharma) surname 'शर्म' (happiness) से आया है। देवी शर्मदायिनी सच्चा सुख देती हैं, temporary pleasure नहीं। Ice cream temporary pleasure है, inner peace शर्म। Netflix binge temporary, meditation शर्म। जब ऐसा सुख मिले जो बिना किसी कारण हो और कभी ख़त्म न हो, वह शर्म है।
modern_context_en: The surname Sharma comes from 'Sharma' (happiness). Devi Sharmadayini gives true happiness, not temporary pleasure. Ice cream is temporary pleasure; inner peace is Sharma. Netflix binge is temporary; meditation is Sharma. When happiness arises without cause and never ends, that is Sharma.
nri_context: When the constant pursuit of pleasure abroad, dining out, shopping, entertainment, finally gives way to a deeper happiness that requires nothing external, Śarmadāyinī has bestowed her true gift.
usage_context: Chant when seeking lasting happiness, during contentment practice, or when distinguishing pleasure from joy.

meaning_en: She who belongs to Shankara (Shiva), the bestower of auspiciousness
meaning_hi: शंकर (शिव, कल्याणकारी) की, शुभता प्रदान करने वाली

śāṅkarī.en: belonging to Shankara (Shiva); she who creates auspiciousness; also: Adi Shankaracharya's lineage-goddess
śāṅkarī.hi: शंकर की; शुभता रचने वाली; आदि शंकराचार्य की वंश-देवी भी

modern_context: शंकर = शं (सुख) + कर (करने वाला) = सुख करने वाला। शंकराचार्य का नाम इसी से है। देवी शाङ्करी शंकर की शक्ति हैं और चार शंकराचार्य मठों (Sringeri, Dwarka, Puri, Jyotirmath) की अधिष्ठात्री। Adi Shankaracharya ने सौन्दर्य लहरी में देवी की स्तुति की। शाङ्करी = Advaita Vedanta की देवी।
modern_context_en: Shankara = Sham (happiness) + Kara (maker) = happiness-maker. Shankaracharya is named from this. Devi Shankari is Shankara's Shakti and presiding deity of four Shankaracharya Mathas (Sringeri, Dwarka, Puri, Jyotirmath). Adi Shankaracharya praised Devi in Saundarya Lahari. Shankari = goddess of Advaita Vedanta.
nri_context: When studying Advaita Vedanta abroad through Shankaracharya's works, Śāṅkarī is the Shakti that powers the lineage connecting you to 1200 years of unbroken philosophical tradition.
usage_context: Chant during Shankaracharya Math events, Advaita study, or during Saundarya Lahari recitation.

meaning_en: She who creates auspiciousness, beauty, and divine grace wherever she turns
meaning_hi: जहाँ भी दृष्टि डालें वहाँ शुभता, सौन्दर्य और कृपा रचने वाली
modern_context: 'श्री' सबसे शुभ prefix है: श्रीराम, श्रीकृष्ण, श्रीमती, श्रीयुत, श्रीलंका (Lanka + Sri)। हर नाम के आगे 'श्री' लगाना उसे शुभ बनाना है। देवी श्रीकरी 'श्री' (auspiciousness) create करती हैं। जब कोई शुभ काम शुरू करें, 'श्री' बोलें। 'श्रीगणेशाय नमः' से शुरू करने की परम्परा इसीलिए।
modern_context_en: 'Sri' is the most auspicious prefix: Sri Rama, Sri Krishna, Srimati, Sri Lanka. Adding 'Sri' before any name makes it auspicious. Devi Srikari creates 'Sri' (auspiciousness). When starting any good work, say 'Sri'. The tradition of beginning with 'Sri Ganeshaya Namah' exists for this reason.
nri_context: When you prefix letters, emails, and documents abroad with 'Sri' or 'Shri', that tiny act invokes Śrīkarī's auspiciousness-creating power into every communication.
usage_context: Chant when starting auspicious work, during Lakshmi puja, or when creating beauty and grace.

meaning_en: She who is the ideal virtuous woman, the perfection of feminine righteousness
meaning_hi: आदर्श सती, स्त्री-धर्म की पूर्णता

sādhvī.en: virtuous woman, chaste, righteous, the feminine ideal of dharma (not restricted to marital chastity but encompassing all feminine virtues)
sādhvī.hi: साध्वी, सती, धार्मिक, सभी स्त्री-गुणों का आदर्श

modern_context: साध्वी = virtuous। यह केवल 'chastity' नहीं, बल्कि सम्पूर्ण स्त्री-धर्म है: करुणा, शक्ति, ज्ञान, धैर्य, उदारता। Savitri जिसने यम से पति की जान बचाई, Draupadi जिसने अन्याय का विरोध किया, Gargi जिसने दार्शनिक शास्त्रार्थ किया, सब साध्वी। साध्वी = dharmic woman, not submissive woman।
modern_context_en: Sadhvi = virtuous. This is not just 'chastity' but complete Stri-Dharma: compassion, strength, knowledge, patience, generosity. Savitri who saved her husband from Yama, Draupadi who protested injustice, Gargi who debated philosophy, all are Sadhvi. Sadhvi = dharmic woman, not submissive woman.
nri_context: When balancing traditional values with modern realities abroad, redefining 'Sadhvi' as a woman of complete virtue, strong, educated, compassionate, and spiritually grounded, rather than merely obedient, honours this name's true meaning.
usage_context: Chant when celebrating feminine virtue, during Savitri Vrat, or when redefining traditional ideals for modern women.

meaning_en: She whose face shines like the autumn full moon, luminous and spotless
meaning_hi: शरद पूर्णिमा के पूर्ण चन्द्रमा जैसा प्रकाशमय और निर्मल मुख वाली

śarat.en: autumn (when the moon is clearest and brightest)

modern_context: शरद पूर्णिमा का चाँद साल का सबसे सुन्दर और सबसे चमकीला होता है। देवी का मुख उसी चाँद जैसा है। शरद पूर्णिमा पर खीर चन्द्रमा की रोशनी में रखकर खाते हैं (चन्द्र किरणों में औषधीय गुण)। कोजागिरी पूर्णिमा और नवरात्रि इसी ऋतु में। शरद = clarity, और देवी का मुख ultimate clarity।
modern_context_en: Sharad Purnima moon is the year's most beautiful and brightest. Devi's face is like that moon. On Sharad Purnima, kheer is placed in moonlight before eating (lunar rays have medicinal properties). Kojagiri Purnima and Navratri fall in this season. Sharad = clarity, and Devi's face is ultimate clarity.
nri_context: When you celebrate Sharad Purnima abroad by placing kheer in moonlight on your balcony, gazing at the same autumn moon your family sees in India, Devi's śaraccandra face shines equally on both hemispheres.
usage_context: Chant during Sharad Purnima, Kojagiri, or during moonlight meditation. Also during Devi's face visualization.

meaning_en: She who has a slender, graceful waist
meaning_hi: पतली और सुडौल कमर वाली
modern_context: शारीरिक वर्णन भारतीय काव्य में शरीर को sacred मानने की परम्परा है। त्रिभंग pose में कमर का सुडौल curve aesthetic का केन्द्र है। देवी की पतली कमर (शातोदरी) शक्ति और सौन्दर्य का संयोग है: thin waist = flexibility + strength। Core strength modern fitness में भी सबसे महत्त्वपूर्ण। शरीर मन्दिर है, उसका सम्मान करें।
modern_context_en: Physical description in Indian poetry reflects the tradition of viewing the body as sacred. In Tribhanga pose the waist's graceful curve is the aesthetic centre. Devi's slender waist (Shatodari) combines power and beauty: thin waist = flexibility + strength. Core strength is paramount in modern fitness too. The body is a temple; respect it.
nri_context: When maintaining physical fitness abroad through yoga, pilates, or core training, you honour the body-temple that Śātodarī's graceful form celebrates.
usage_context: Chant during body-positive meditation, core-strengthening practice, or during Devi's physical-form visualization.

meaning_en: She who is full of peace and who radiates tranquillity to all beings
meaning_hi: शान्ति से परिपूर्ण और सभी प्राणियों को शान्ति विकीर्ण करने वाली
modern_context: 'ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः' तीन बार शान्ति बोलने का अर्थ: आधिभौतिक (physical), आधिदैविक (divine/natural), आध्यात्मिक (spiritual) तीन प्रकार के दुःखों की शान्ति। WHO Mental Health report कहता है anxiety और depression epidemic हैं। देवी शान्तिमती inner peace का स्रोत हैं। Meditation = Shanti practice।
modern_context_en: Om Shantih Shantih Shantih: three Shantis for three types of suffering: Adhibhautika (physical), Adhidaivika (natural), Adhyatmika (spiritual). WHO Mental Health report says anxiety and depression are epidemics. Devi Shantimati is the source of inner peace. Meditation = Shanti practice.
nri_context: When the three-fold stress abroad, physical exhaustion, environmental challenges, and spiritual emptiness, overwhelms you, Śāntimatī's triple peace (om shantih shantih shantih) addresses all three simultaneously.
usage_context: Chant during Shanti Mantra, stress relief, or during World Mental Health Day. Also during any Vedic conclusion ceremony.

meaning_en: She who needs no support, the foundation of all yet founded on nothing
meaning_hi: किसी आधार (सहारे) की आवश्यकता न रखने वाली, सबका आधार लेकिन स्वयं निराधार
modern_context: Earth पर सब कुछ gravity से टिका, gravity space-time curvature पर, space-time Big Bang पर, Big Bang पर? कुछ नहीं। Ultimate foundation (देवी) स्वयं किसी foundation पर नहीं। 'Atlas holds the Earth, but who holds Atlas?' का उत्तर: कोई नहीं, Atlas (देवी) स्वयं निराधार है। Self-sustaining = निराधार।
modern_context_en: On Earth everything rests on gravity, gravity on space-time curvature, space-time on Big Bang, Big Bang on? Nothing. The ultimate foundation (Devi) rests on no foundation herself. 'Atlas holds Earth, but who holds Atlas?' Answer: nobody; Atlas (Devi) is self-sustaining. Self-sustaining = Niradhara.
nri_context: When every external support abroad, job, visa, community - feels precarious and you wonder what your ultimate foundation is, Nirādhārā answers: the real foundation needs no foundation. Trust the foundationless ground of being.
usage_context: Chant during groundlessness meditation, when all supports seem unstable, or during ultimate trust practice.

meaning_en: She who is stainless and untouched by any impurity or coloring
meaning_hi: निर्मल, किसी अशुद्धता या रंग (अंजन) से अस्पर्शित
modern_context: अंजन = काजल = colouring। निरंजन = बिना किसी colouring/stain। देवी निरंजना पर कोई दाग़ नहीं लगता। कमल पानी में रहकर भी गीला नहीं, देवी संसार में रहकर भी अस्पर्शित। जब ईमानदार रहें भ्रष्ट environment में भी, जब सकारात्मक रहें negative surroundings में, वह निरंजन भाव। Teflon coating = निरंजन। कुछ चिपकता नहीं।
modern_context_en: Anjana = collyrium = colouring. Niranjana = without any stain. Nothing stains Devi. A lotus in water stays dry; Devi in the world stays untouched. When remaining honest in a corrupt environment, positive in negative surroundings, that is Niranjana spirit. Teflon coating = Niranjana. Nothing sticks.
nri_context: When the moral compromises of immigrant survival abroad, small lies, ethical grey areas - tempt you and you choose to remain nirañjana (stainless), that integrity is this name's power in action.
usage_context: Chant when maintaining integrity, during purity meditation, or when resisting moral compromise.

meaning_en: She to whom nothing clings, free from all attachment and karmic residue
meaning_hi: जिनसे कुछ नहीं चिपकता, सभी आसक्ति और कर्म-अवशेष से मुक्त
modern_context: लेप = coating/clinging। निर्लेप = nothing clings। कर्म करो लेकिन कर्म-फल 'चिपके' नहीं। गीता का 'निष्काम कर्म' = निर्लेप कर्म। जब काम करें और result 'चिपके' नहीं (न success का घमण्ड, न failure का दुःख), वह निर्लेप। Non-stick pan = निर्लेप। Food (karma) बनता है लेकिन pan (self) पर कुछ नहीं चिपकता।
modern_context_en: Lepa = coating/clinging. Nirlepa = nothing clings. Do karma but let karma-phala not 'stick'. Gita's 'Nishkama Karma' = Nirlepa Karma. When working and results do not 'stick' (neither pride in success nor grief in failure), that is Nirlepa. Non-stick pan = Nirlepa. Food (karma) is cooked but nothing sticks to the pan (self).
nri_context: When you perform your duties abroad excellently yet remain unattached to outcomes, neither elated by praise nor crushed by criticism, you embody the nirlepā non-stick quality this name celebrates.
usage_context: Chant during Nishkama Karma practice, Gita Chapter 3 study, or when cultivating non-attachment to results.

meaning_en: She who is absolutely pure, spotless, and without any blemish
meaning_hi: पूर्णतः शुद्ध, निर्मल, बिना किसी दोष
modern_context: निर्मला = बिना मल (impurity)। तीन मल (impurities) हैं: आणव मल (smallness/ego), मायीय मल (delusion), कार्म मल (karma residue)। देवी तीनों से मुक्त हैं। Nirmal (clean) India campaign, Nirmala Sitharaman (Finance Minister), Nirmal Baba, सब इसी शब्द से। जब मन शुद्ध हो, विचार साफ़ हों, intention pure हो, वह निर्मल अवस्था।
modern_context_en: Nirmala = without impurity. Three Malas (impurities) exist: Anava Mala (ego), Mayiya Mala (delusion), Karma Mala (karmic residue). Devi is free from all three. Nirmal (clean) India campaign, Nirmala Sitharaman, all from this word. When mind is pure, thoughts clear, intentions clean, that is Nirmala state.
nri_context: When your intentions abroad are pure, your dealings transparent, and your conscience clear, that nirmalā state of inner cleanliness is the most authentic worship of this name.
usage_context: Chant during purification practices, when seeking mental clarity, or during Mala-Shuddhi (impurity-cleansing) meditation.

meaning_en: She who is eternal, permanent, and ever-present without beginning or end
meaning_hi: शाश्वत, स्थायी, और सदा विद्यमान, बिना आदि-अन्त

nityā.en: eternal, permanent, ever-present; also: the 16 Nitya Devis (eternal goddesses)

modern_context: सब कुछ अनित्य (impermanent) है: शरीर, सम्बन्ध, सम्पत्ति, यहाँ तक कि तारे भी। लेकिन देवी नित्य (eternal) हैं। बुद्ध ने 'अनित्य' सिखाया, हिन्दू दर्शन 'नित्य' सिखाता है: अनित्य के पीछे नित्य (आत्मा/देवी) है। दोनों सत्य हैं: बाहरी अनित्य, भीतरी नित्य। 16 Nitya Devis चन्द्रमा की 16 कलाएँ हैं।
modern_context_en: Everything is Anitya (impermanent): body, relationships, wealth, even stars. But Devi is Nitya (eternal). Buddha taught 'Anitya'; Hindu philosophy teaches 'Nitya': behind the impermanent is the permanent (soul/Devi). Both truths coexist: outer impermanent, inner eternal. 16 Nitya Devis are the moon's 16 phases.
nri_context: When everything abroad feels impermanent, jobs, visas, friendships, homes, this name anchors you: behind all transience, Nityā Devi is the unchanging permanence that your soul shares.
usage_context: Chant during Nitya-Anitya contemplation, 16-Nitya worship, or when impermanence creates anxiety.

meaning_en: She who is formless, beyond all shape and appearance
meaning_hi: निराकार, सभी आकार और रूप से परे
modern_context: देवी साकार (with form) भी हैं और निराकार (formless) भी। मूर्ति-पूजा = साकार उपासना। ध्यान = निराकार उपासना। दोनों valid। जब beginner हों, साकार (murti, photo) ज़रूरी। जब advanced हों, निराकार (formless awareness) sufficient। Electricity formless है लेकिन bulb में form लेती है, वैसे ही देवी formless हैं लेकिन murti में form लेती हैं।
modern_context_en: Devi is both Sakara (with form) and Nirakara (formless). Murti-puja = Sakara worship. Meditation = Nirakara worship. Both valid. Beginners need Sakara (murti, photo). Advanced practitioners find Nirakara (formless awareness) sufficient. Electricity is formless but takes form in a bulb; similarly Devi is formless but takes form in a murti.
nri_context: When no murti or temple is available abroad, formless meditation connects you to Nirākārā Devi directly, requiring nothing but your awareness. The most portable form of worship.
usage_context: Chant during Nirguna meditation, when forms are unavailable, or during Saguna-Nirguna philosophical discussion.

meaning_en: She who is completely unperturbed, free from all agitation and confusion
meaning_hi: पूर्णतः अविचलित, सभी उद्वेग और भ्रम से मुक्त
modern_context: आकुल = anxious/agitated। निराकुल = anxiety-free। WHO कहता है 280 million+ लोग depression से और 300 million+ anxiety से पीड़ित। देवी निराकुला पूर्ण शान्ति में हैं। जब anxiety attack आए, deep breathe करें और 'निराकुला' बोलें। मन को command दें: 'तू निराकुल हो जा।' शब्द में शक्ति है।
modern_context_en: Akula = anxious/agitated. Nirakula = anxiety-free. WHO says 280M+ people suffer depression and 300M+ anxiety. Devi Nirakula is in perfect peace. During an anxiety attack, breathe deep and say 'Nirakula'. Command the mind: 'become unperturbed.' Words carry power.
nri_context: When anxiety abroad escalates, immigration worries, financial stress, family concerns, all piling up, chanting 'Nirākulā' as a conscious command to your nervous system initiates the divine calm Devi embodies.
usage_context: Chant during anxiety, panic attacks, or when mental agitation needs calming. One of the most therapeutically practical names.

meaning_en: She who transcends all three gunas (Sattva, Rajas, Tamas)
meaning_hi: तीनों गुणों (सत्त्व, रजस्, तमस्) से परे

guṇā.en: gunas (the three fundamental qualities of nature: Sattva/purity, Rajas/activity, Tamas/inertia)
guṇā.hi: गुण (प्रकृति के तीन मूल गुण: सत्त्व, रजस्, तमस्)

modern_context: तीन गुण प्रकृति के fundamental modes हैं: सत्त्व (पवित्रता), रजस् (गतिविधि), तमस् (जड़ता)। सब कुछ इन तीनों का combination है। लेकिन देवी तीनों से परे (निर्गुण) हैं। Kabir ने 'निर्गुण' ब्रह्म की स्तुति की। Sant tradition (Kabir, Nanak, Ravidas) निर्गुण उपासना करती है। देवी सगुण भी हैं (with form/qualities) और निर्गुण भी (beyond)।
modern_context_en: Three Gunas are nature's fundamental modes: Sattva (purity), Rajas (activity), Tamas (inertia). Everything is a combination of these three. But Devi transcends all three (Nirguna). Kabir praised 'Nirguna' Brahman. The Sant tradition (Kabir, Nanak, Ravidas) practises Nirguna worship. Devi is both Saguna (with qualities) and Nirguna (beyond).
nri_context: When the pendulum of immigrant life abroad swings between sattvic discipline, rajasic ambition, and tamasic exhaustion, Nirguṇā Devi reminds you of the witness-awareness beyond all three modes.
usage_context: Chant during Gita Chapter 14 study, Nirguna meditation, or during Sant tradition kirtan.

meaning_en: She who is without parts, indivisible and whole
meaning_hi: अंशरहित, अविभाज्य और सम्पूर्ण
modern_context: निष्कल = बिना अंशों (parts) के। Atom को 'अ-टॉम' (indivisible) कहा गया लेकिन बाद में divide हुआ। Quark को fundamental particle कहते हैं लेकिन शायद वह भी divisible। देवी सच में निष्कल (indivisible) हैं: उन्हें किसी भी तरह divide नहीं किया जा सकता। जब कहें 'मैं body, mind, soul हूँ', देवी कहती हैं 'मैं बस हूँ, undivided।'
modern_context_en: Nishkala = without parts. Atom was called 'a-tom' (indivisible) but was later divided. Quarks are called fundamental particles but may also be divisible. Devi is truly Nishkala (indivisible): she cannot be divided in any way. When saying 'I am body, mind, soul', Devi says 'I just AM, undivided.'
nri_context: When immigrant identity abroad fragments you into 'Indian self', 'professional self', 'parent self', 'spiritual self', Niṣkalā's teaching is: you are not parts assembled but one indivisible wholeness.
usage_context: Chant during wholeness meditation, when identity fragmentation occurs, or during Advaita study.

meaning_en: She who is peace itself, the embodiment of absolute tranquillity
meaning_hi: शान्ति स्वरूपा, परम प्रशान्ति का स्वरूप

śāntā.en: peaceful, tranquil, at rest, the embodiment of peace

modern_context: शान्ता = peace personified। नवरस (nine emotions) में शान्त रस (peace) सबसे ऊँचा माना जाता है (Abhinavagupta के अनुसार)। सब रसों (शृंगार, हास्य, करुणा, वीर, भयानक, रौद्र, अद्भुत, बीभत्स) का destination शान्त रस है। Films, music, life सब शान्ति पर end होने चाहिए। 'और अन्त में शान्ति।'
modern_context_en: Shanta = peace personified. Among Navarasa (nine emotions), Shanta Rasa (peace) is highest (per Abhinavagupta). All rasas (Shringara, Hasya, Karuna, Veera, Bhayanaka, Raudra, Adbhuta, Bibhatsa) have Shanta Rasa as their destination. Films, music, life should all end in peace. 'And finally, Shanti.'
nri_context: When the drama of immigrant life abroad, its thrills, fears, joys, sorrows - ultimately resolves into acceptance and inner quietude, you have reached Śāntā, the final rasa where all stories end in peace.
usage_context: Chant during peace meditation, at life's culminating moments, or during Navarasa-Shanta study.

meaning_en: She who is free from all desires, the desireless absolute
meaning_hi: सभी इच्छाओं से मुक्त, निष्काम परम तत्त्व
modern_context: निष्काम = desireless। गीता का central teaching 'निष्काम कर्म' (desireless action) = काम करो लेकिन फल की इच्छा मत रखो। देवी निष्कामा हैं: उन्हें कुछ नहीं चाहिए। जो पूर्ण है उसे क्या चाहिए? जब इच्छाओं का भार उतारें और 'बस है' की शान्ति में बैठें, निष्काम अवस्था उभरती है। Minimalism = practical निष्काम।
modern_context_en: Nishkama = desireless. Gita's central teaching 'Nishkama Karma' (desireless action) = work but do not desire the fruit. Devi is Nishkama: she wants nothing. What does the Complete One need? When the burden of desires is dropped and 'enough' peace is found, the Nishkama state emerges. Minimalism = practical Nishkama.
nri_context: When the immigrant desire-cycle abroad, bigger house, better car, higher salary - is seen through and you find peace in 'enough', that is Niṣkāmā's desireless freedom descending upon your consciousness.
usage_context: Chant during Nishkama Karma practice, minimalism, or when the cycle of desire needs breaking.

meaning_en: She who is free from all calamity, disturbance, and cosmic upheaval
meaning_hi: सभी उपद्रव, विपत्ति और ब्रह्माण्डीय उथल-पुथल से मुक्त
modern_context: उपप्लव = catastrophe। COVID, tsunamis, earthquakes, wars सब उपप्लव हैं। देवी इन सबसे अप्रभावित हैं। जब ब्रह्माण्ड में सब उथल-पुथल हो, देवी शान्त। जैसे तूफ़ान की आँख (eye of the storm) शान्त होती है, देवी cosmic storms की आँख हैं। जब जीवन में upheaval हो, देवी की शान्ति में refuge लें।
modern_context_en: Upaplava = catastrophe. COVID, tsunamis, earthquakes, wars are all upaplava. Devi is unaffected by all. When the cosmos is in turmoil, Devi is calm. Like the eye of a storm is peaceful, Devi is the eye of cosmic storms. During life's upheavals, take refuge in Devi's calm.
nri_context: When global crises abroad, pandemics, economic crashes, political upheavals - create chaos all around, Nirupaplavā is the divine calm at the centre of every storm. Take refuge there.
usage_context: Chant during crises, natural disasters, or when seeking the calm centre within chaos. A fitting 940th name marking the final stretch of this sacred journey.

meaning_en: She who is eternally liberated, never having been bound in the first place
meaning_hi: शाश्वत रूप से मुक्त, जो कभी बँधी ही नहीं
modern_context: नित्यमुक्त = सदा मुक्त। हम 'मुक्ति पानी है' कहते हैं, लेकिन देवी को 'पानी' नहीं क्योंकि वे कभी बँधी ही नहीं। जो बँधा नहीं उसे मुक्ति कैसी? यह सबसे गहन teaching है: आपकी आत्मा भी नित्यमुक्त है, आप कभी बँधे ही नहीं, बस भूल गए। अज्ञान = बन्धन का भ्रम। ज्ञान = 'मैं सदा मुक्त था' का बोध।
modern_context_en: Nityamukta = eternally free. We say 'attain liberation' but Devi does not need to 'attain' it because she was never bound. How can the unbound be liberated? This is the deepest teaching: your soul too is Nityamukta; you were never bound, just forgot. Ignorance = illusion of bondage. Knowledge = realization 'I was always free.'
nri_context: When the sense of being 'trapped' abroad, by visa, by job, by circumstances - feels suffocating, this name whispers the ultimate truth: your essential self was never bound. The cage is imaginary.
usage_context: Chant during Advaita realization, when bondage feels overwhelming, or during 'who am I?' self-inquiry. The most liberating name in the final stretch.

meaning_en: She who is free from all modifications, changes, and distortions
meaning_hi: सभी विकार (परिवर्तन, विकृति) से मुक्त
modern_context: शरीर में छह विकार हैं: जायते (जन्म), अस्ति (अस्तित्व), वर्धते (वृद्धि), विपरिणमते (परिवर्तन), अपक्षीयते (क्षय), विनश्यति (नाश)। देवी इन छह विकारों से मुक्त। Ageing, illness, mood-swings, सब विकार हैं। देवी में कोई modification नहीं: वे जैसी अरबों साल पहले थीं, वैसी आज भी, वैसी अरबों साल बाद भी।
modern_context_en: The body has six Vikaras: birth, existence, growth, transformation, decay, death. Devi is free from all six. Ageing, illness, mood-swings are all Vikaras. Devi has no modification: she is the same as billions of years ago, today, and billions of years hence.
nri_context: When the constant modifications of immigrant life, adapting, changing, adjusting, evolving - exhaust you, connecting to the nirvikārā unchanging Devi within provides the stability no external change can shake.
usage_context: Chant during change-fatigue, when seeking the unchanging, or during Shad-Vikara (six modifications) study.

meaning_en: She who is beyond the entire phenomenal world of appearances
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण प्रपंच (दृश्य जगत्, नाम-रूप का विस्तार) से परे

prapañcā.en: the phenomenal world, the manifest universe of names and forms, worldly expansion
prapañcā.hi: प्रपंच, दृश्य जगत्, नाम-रूप का विस्तार

modern_context: प्रपंच = 'पंच भूतों से बना विस्तार', अर्थात् physical universe। देवी इससे परे। Mandukya Karika कहता है 'प्रपंचोपशमं शान्तं शिवमद्वैतं चतुर्थं मन्यन्ते', प्रपंच का उपशम (quieting) ही तुरीय (Devi) है। जब meditation में बाहरी दुनिया fade हो जाए और केवल awareness बचे, प्रपंच-उपशम हो रहा है।
modern_context_en: Prapancha = 'expansion made of five elements', meaning the physical universe. Devi is beyond this. Mandukya Karika says 'Prapanchopashamam Shantam Shivam Advaitam Chaturtham Manyante', the quieting of Prapancha IS Turiya (Devi). When in meditation the outer world fades and only awareness remains, Prapancha-Upashama is occurring.
nri_context: When the sensory overload of life abroad, screens, noise, crowds, information - is temporarily switched off in deep meditation, and pure awareness remains, you touch the niṣprapañca reality beyond all phenomena.
usage_context: Chant during deep withdrawal meditation, Mandukya Karika study, or when the world needs to be set aside for truth.

meaning_en: She who depends on no refuge, being herself the ultimate refuge of all
meaning_hi: किसी आश्रय पर निर्भर नहीं, स्वयं सबका परम आश्रय
modern_context: सब कुछ किसी आश्रय पर निर्भर है: मनुष्य मकान पर, मछली पानी पर, पक्षी पेड़ पर। लेकिन देवी किसी आश्रय पर निर्भर नहीं और सबका आश्रय स्वयं हैं। 'शरणागति' (surrender/taking refuge) भक्ति का सर्वोच्च act है और देवी वह ultimate refuge। जब सब doors बन्द हों, देवी का दरवाज़ा सदा खुला।
modern_context_en: Everything depends on some refuge: humans on houses, fish on water, birds on trees. But Devi depends on no refuge and is herself everyone's refuge. 'Sharanagati' (surrender) is bhakti's highest act and Devi is the ultimate refuge. When all doors close, Devi's door is always open.
nri_context: When every support system abroad fails, friends unavailable, family far, institutions unhelpful, Nirāśrayā Devi remains the one refuge that never turns anyone away, never closes, never fails.
usage_context: Chant during Sharanagati (surrender), when all other refuges fail, or during ultimate trust practice.

meaning_en: She who is eternally pure, never having been touched by any impurity
meaning_hi: शाश्वत रूप से शुद्ध, किसी अशुद्धि से कभी अस्पर्शित
modern_context: देवी को 'शुद्ध करने' की ज़रूरत नहीं क्योंकि वे 'अशुद्ध' हुई ही नहीं। जैसे आकाश में बादल आते-जाते हैं लेकिन आकाश गन्दा नहीं होता, वैसे ही संसार के विकार देवी को अशुद्ध नहीं करते। आपकी आत्मा भी नित्यशुद्ध है। बुरे कर्म मन और शरीर को अशुद्ध करते हैं, आत्मा को नहीं।
modern_context_en: Devi does not need 'purification' because she was never 'impure'. Like clouds come and go but the sky is not dirtied, worldly distortions do not make Devi impure. Your soul too is Nityashuddha. Bad deeds make mind and body impure, not the soul.
nri_context: When guilt from past mistakes abroad makes you feel permanently tainted, this name reveals: your essential self is nityaśuddha. Actions may have consequences, but your core purity is untouched.
usage_context: Chant during purification, when guilt overwhelms, or during Atma-Shuddhi (soul-purity) meditation.

meaning_en: She who is eternally awake and aware, never subject to ignorance
meaning_hi: शाश्वत रूप से जागृत और जागरूक, अज्ञान से कभी अधीन नहीं

buddhā.hi: बुद्ध, जागृत (बुद्ध शब्द का वही मूल)

modern_context: नित्यबुद्ध = eternally awake। 'बुद्ध' शब्द 'बुध्' (to awaken) से आता है। Gautama Buddha एक जन्म में awakened हुए, देवी सदा से awakened हैं। Ignorance उन्हें कभी छुआ नहीं। जब AI 'artificial intelligence' develop करे, देवी 'eternal intelligence' हैं जिसे develop नहीं करना पड़ा, वह सदा से है।
modern_context_en: Nityabuddha = eternally awake. 'Buddha' comes from 'Budh' (to awaken). Gautama Buddha awakened in one lifetime; Devi has been awake forever. Ignorance never touched her. When AI develops 'artificial intelligence', Devi is 'eternal intelligence' that never needed development; it always existed.
nri_context: When knowledge gained abroad through education, experience, and study feels like 'waking up', that awakening is a small reflection of Nityabuddhā's eternal awareness that never needed any alarm clock.
usage_context: Chant during Buddha Purnima, awakening meditation, or during Buddhist-Hindu dialogue on the nature of enlightenment.

meaning_en: She who is beyond all fault, blame, and criticism
meaning_hi: सभी दोष, निन्दा और आलोचना से परे
modern_context: हर मनुष्य में दोष (faults) हैं, लेकिन देवी निरवद्य (faultless) हैं। जब कोई किसी में fault ढूँढे, वह मनुष्य-स्वभाव है। लेकिन देवी में fault ढूँढना असम्भव। Code review में bugs मिलते हैं, लेकिन देवी का 'code' bug-free है। Perfect software = impossible, Perfect deity = Niravadya Devi।
modern_context_en: Every human has faults but Devi is Niravadya (faultless). When someone finds fault in others, that is human nature. But finding fault in Devi is impossible. Code review finds bugs but Devi's 'code' is bug-free. Perfect software = impossible; Perfect deity = Niravadya Devi.
nri_context: When criticism abroad, for your accent, your culture, your choices - stings deeply, this name offers a mirror: your essential divine nature is niravadyā, beyond any fault anyone can project onto you.
usage_context: Chant when handling criticism, during self-acceptance, or when perfectionism becomes oppressive.

meaning_en: She who is continuous, without any gap or interruption, seamlessly present
meaning_hi: निरन्तर, बिना किसी अन्तराल या बाधा, सहज रूप से उपस्थित
modern_context: निरन्तर = continuous, without breaks। Internet connection 'continuous' चाहते हैं लेकिन buffering होता है। देवी की उपस्थिति में कोई buffering नहीं, कोई signal loss नहीं। 24/7, 365 days, अनन्त काल तक continuous। जब लगे 'देवी से disconnect हो गया', असल में connection कभी टूटा नहीं, बस awareness हटी।
modern_context_en: Nirantara = continuous, without breaks. We want 'continuous' internet but buffering happens. Devi's presence has no buffering, no signal loss. 24/7, 365 days, for eternity continuous. When feeling 'disconnected from Devi', the connection never broke; only awareness shifted.
nri_context: When the distance between India and abroad makes Devi feel far away, Nirantarā reminds: her presence has zero latency, zero buffering, zero disconnection. The divine signal is always full-strength.
usage_context: Chant when feeling spiritually disconnected, during continuous-awareness practice, or during Sakshi meditation.

meaning_en: She who is causeless, having no cause behind her own existence
meaning_hi: अकारण, जिसके अस्तित्व का कोई कारण नहीं
modern_context: हर चीज़ का कारण है: बीमारी का virus, बारिश का evaporation, day का rotation। लेकिन देवी का कोई कारण नहीं। 'देवी क्यों हैं?' का उत्तर: 'कोई कारण नहीं, बस हैं।' First Cause philosophy में Uncaused Cause = God। देवी वह Uncaused Cause हैं। 'Why does anything exist at all?' Because Nishkarana Devi IS।
modern_context_en: Everything has a cause: disease has a virus, rain has evaporation, day has rotation. But Devi has no cause. 'Why does Devi exist?' Answer: 'No reason; she just IS.' In First Cause philosophy, the Uncaused Cause = God. Devi is that Uncaused Cause. 'Why does anything exist at all?' Because Nishkarana Devi IS.
nri_context: When the existential question 'why am I here?' becomes acute abroad, this name points to a liberating answer: Devi exists for no reason, and your deepest self shares that causeless, purposeless, joyful being.
usage_context: Chant during existential contemplation, First Cause philosophy, or during 'why does anything exist?' meditation.

meaning_en: She who is without any blemish, stain, or mark of imperfection
meaning_hi: किसी कलंक, दाग़ या अपूर्णता के चिह्न से रहित
modern_context: चन्द्रमा में कलंक (dark spots/craters) हैं लेकिन देवी में कोई कलंक नहीं। 'निष्कलंक' Kalki avatar (विष्णु का अन्तिम अवतार) का विशेषण भी है। समाज में 'कलंक' (stigma) लगाना सबसे cruel act है: divorce stigma, mental health stigma, caste stigma। देवी निष्कलंका stigma-free हैं और भक्तों को भी stigma से मुक्त करती हैं।
modern_context_en: The moon has blemishes (dark spots/craters) but Devi has none. 'Nishkalanka' is also an epithet of Kalki avatar. Society's 'kalanka' (stigma) is the cruelest act: divorce stigma, mental health stigma, caste stigma. Devi is Nishkalanka, stigma-free, and frees devotees from stigma too.
nri_context: When social stigma from the Indian community abroad, for divorce, for unconventional choices, for seeking therapy - weighs heavily, Niṣkalaṅkā Devi models the blemish-free state and frees you from others' judgements.
usage_context: Chant when facing stigma, during anti-stigma advocacy, or when seeking freedom from social blemish. The 950th name marks 95% completion.

meaning_en: She who is without any limiting adjunct or qualifying condition
meaning_hi: किसी सीमित उपाधि या योग्यता-शर्त से रहित

upādhiḥ.en: limiting adjunct, qualifying condition, title, label (in Vedanta: that which falsely limits the infinite)
upādhiḥ.hi: उपाधि, सीमित करने वाली शर्त, लेबल

modern_context: उपाधि = limiting label। 'मैं Indian हूँ', 'मैं engineer हूँ', 'मैं माँ हूँ', सब उपाधियाँ हैं जो असीम आत्मा को सीमित करती हैं। देवी किसी उपाधि से सीमित नहीं: वे Hindu नहीं, Indian नहीं, female भी नहीं (they transcend gender), बस 'हैं'। जब सब labels हटें, जो बचे वह निरुपाधि सत्य।
modern_context_en: Upadhi = limiting label. 'I am Indian', 'I am an engineer', 'I am a mother', all are Upadhis limiting the infinite soul. Devi is limited by no Upadhi: not Hindu, not Indian, not even female (she transcends gender); she just IS. When all labels are removed, what remains is Nirupadhi truth.
nri_context: When the accumulation of labels abroad, immigrant, NRI, H1B, citizen, taxpayer - defines and confines you, Nirupādhiḥ invites you to discover the label-free awareness beneath every identity.
usage_context: Chant during identity-dissolution meditation, when labels become prisons, or during Vedanta study on Upadhi.

meaning_en: She who has no lord above her, the supreme sovereign with no superior
meaning_hi: जिनके ऊपर कोई ईश्वर (स्वामी) नहीं, बिना किसी superior सर्वोच्च
modern_context: नीरीश्वरा = no lord above her। PM के ऊपर President, President के ऊपर Constitution, Constitution के ऊपर? देवी। लेकिन देवी के ऊपर कोई नहीं। CEO board को report करता है, board shareholders को, shareholders market को, market को? देवी। Ultimate authority chain देवी पर end होती है। Buck stops here = Neerishvara।
modern_context_en: Neerishvara = no lord above her. Above PM is President, above President is Constitution, above Constitution? Devi. But above Devi is nobody. CEO reports to board, board to shareholders, shareholders to market, market to? Devi. The ultimate authority chain ends at Devi. 'The buck stops here' = Neerishvara.
nri_context: When navigating complex authority structures abroad, bosses, immigration officers, legal systems, this name grounds you: above every earthly authority sits Nīrīśvarā, the one authority that answers to none.
usage_context: Chant when asserting ultimate divine authority, during power-hierarchy contemplation, or when earthly authorities seem overwhelming.

meaning_en: She who is free from all passionate attachment and craving
meaning_hi: सभी आसक्ति और तृष्णा से मुक्त

āgā.en: attachment, passion, craving, desire, also: musical raga (passionate melody)

modern_context: राग = attachment/passion। Eternal Raga app का नाम 'raga' (शाश्वत राग) से है, लेकिन देवी स्वयं नीरागा (beyond raga) हैं! Paradox: वे राग का स्रोत हैं लेकिन स्वयं राग-मुक्त। जैसे fire गर्मी देती है लेकिन स्वयं 'गर्म' feel नहीं करती, देवी आसक्ति create करती हैं लेकिन स्वयं attached नहीं।
modern_context_en: Raga = attachment/passion. Eternal Raga app is named from 'Raga' (eternal melody), but Devi herself is Niraga (beyond raga)! Paradox: she is the source of raga but herself raga-free. Like fire gives heat but does not 'feel' hot herself, Devi creates attachment but is herself unattached.
nri_context: When attachment to outcomes abroad, visa results, job offers, relationship goals - creates suffering, this name models the alternative: being the source of passionate engagement while remaining inwardly nīrāga (detached).
usage_context: Chant during Vairagya (detachment) practice, when passion becomes possessive, or during Raga-Dvesha contemplation. A beautifully ironic name for Eternal Raga's context.

meaning_en: She who churns and destroys the passion of worldly attachment
meaning_hi: सांसारिक आसक्ति के राग (passion) को मथकर नष्ट करने वाली

mathanī.en: churner, destroyer (like the churning rod of Samudra Manthana)
mathanī.hi: मथने वाली (समुद्र मन्थन की मथानी जैसे)

modern_context: मथन = churning। समुद्र मन्थन में poison (हालाहल) और nectar (अमृत) दोनों निकले। जब देवी राग (attachment) को मथती हैं, poison (दुःख) निकलता है और amrit (शान्ति) बचता है। Detox = modern churning। जब toxic relationships, habits, या addictions 'churn' हों और निकलें, वह painful है लेकिन healing।
modern_context_en: Mathana = churning. In Samudra Manthana, both poison (Halahala) and nectar (Amrita) emerged. When Devi churns raga (attachment), poison (suffering) comes out and amrita (peace) remains. Detox = modern churning. When toxic relationships, habits, or addictions are 'churned' out, it is painful but healing.
nri_context: When a painful process abroad forces you to release deep attachments, letting go of a toxic relationship, quitting an addiction, leaving a comfortable but stagnant job, Rāgamathanī is churning your attachments to extract the amrita of freedom.
usage_context: Chant during detox, painful letting-go, or during Samudra Manthana metaphorical meditation.

meaning_en: She who is free from all intoxication of pride and arrogance
meaning_hi: गर्व और अहंकार के नशे से पूर्णतः मुक्त

madā.en: intoxication, pride, arrogance, madness of ego

modern_context: मद = intoxication/pride। अष्ट मद (eight prides) हैं: कुल (lineage), शील (character), रूप (beauty), विद्या (learning), धन (wealth), बल (strength), यौवन (youth), सत्ता (power)। देवी जो सबकी स्वामिनी हैं उनमें कोई मद नहीं। जब Nobel Prize जीतकर भी विनम्र रहें, करोड़पति होकर भी सादा रहें, वह निर्मद।
modern_context_en: Mada = intoxication/pride. Eight Madas exist: lineage, character, beauty, learning, wealth, strength, youth, power. Devi who owns everything has no Mada. When winning a Nobel Prize yet remaining humble, being a billionaire yet living simply, that is Nirmada.
nri_context: When success abroad inflates the ego through any of the eight prides, this name is the antidote: Devi possesses ALL eight in infinite measure yet remains utterly nirmadā. True power needs no arrogance.
usage_context: Chant during Ashta-Mada (eight prides) study, when ego surfaces after success, or during humility practice.

meaning_en: She who destroys the intoxication of ego and arrogance in her devotees
meaning_hi: भक्तों में अहंकार के नशे को नष्ट करने वाली
modern_context: पिछला नाम (955) कहता है देवी स्वयं मद-मुक्त, यह कहता है देवी दूसरों का मद भी मिटाती हैं। जब अहंकारी को कोई humbling experience हो, जब 'मैं कुछ भी कर सकता हूँ' वाले को असफलता मिले, जब power-drunk leader को जनता reject करे, वह मदनाशिनी देवी का कार्य। Ego destruction painful है लेकिन necessary।
modern_context_en: Previous name (955) says Devi is pride-free; this says Devi destroys others' pride too. When an arrogant person has a humbling experience, when 'I can do anything' meets failure, when a power-drunk leader is rejected by people, that is Madanashini Devi's work. Ego destruction is painful but necessary.
nri_context: When a humbling experience abroad, a public failure, an unexpected rejection, a plan that collapses, shatters your ego, Madanāśinī is performing surgery: painful now, healing forever.
usage_context: Chant when ego needs breaking, after humbling experiences, or during Ahamkara (ego) dissolution meditation.

meaning_en: She who is completely free from all worry and anxious thought
meaning_hi: सभी चिन्ता और व्यग्र विचारों से पूर्णतः मुक्त
modern_context: चिन्ता (worry) आज epidemic है। 'चिन्ता चिता समानास्ति' (worry is like a funeral pyre) कहावत है। गीता कहती है 'मा शुचः' (चिन्ता मत करो)। देवी निश्चिन्ता हैं: उन्हें कोई worry नहीं। अगर ब्रह्माण्ड की मालकिन को कोई चिन्ता नहीं, तो आपको क्यों? जब चिन्ता आए, 'निश्चिन्ता' बोलें और देवी को सौंप दें।
modern_context_en: Chinta (worry) is epidemic today. 'Chinta Chita Samanasti' (worry is like a funeral pyre) is a proverb. Gita says 'Ma Shuchah' (do not worry). Devi is Nishchinta: she has no worry. If the cosmos's owner has no worry, why should you? When worry comes, say 'Nishchinta' and surrender it to Devi.
nri_context: When the worry-list abroad, visa, bills, children's future, parents' health, career, retirement - grows endless, handing each item to Niścintā Devi lightens the load one worry at a time.
usage_context: Chant during anxiety, when worry spirals, or when 'Ma Shuchah' from the Gita needs embodiment.

meaning_en: She who is completely free from ego, the I-sense, and self-importance
meaning_hi: अहंकार ('मैं' भाव, आत्म-महत्व) से पूर्णतः मुक्त
modern_context: अहंकार = 'अहं' (I) + 'कार' (maker) = 'I-maker', वह शक्ति जो 'मैं' का भ्रम बनाती है। देवी निरहंकारा हैं: उनमें 'मैं' का कोई भ्रम नहीं। 'मैं ब्रह्माण्ड चलाती हूँ' ऐसा अहंकार भी नहीं। CEO जो 'मैं company चलाता हूँ' न बोले बल्कि 'company team से चलती है' बोले, वह निरहंकारी।
modern_context_en: Ahamkara = 'Aham' (I) + 'Kara' (maker) = the 'I-maker', the power creating the illusion of 'I'. Devi is Nirahankara: no illusion of 'I'. Not even the ego of 'I run the cosmos'. A CEO who says 'the company runs because of the team' rather than 'I run the company' is Nirahankari.
nri_context: When achievements abroad tempt you to think 'I did this alone', Nirahaṃkārā gently dissolves the 'I' and reveals the web of grace, help, luck, and Devi-Shakti that actually made it possible.
usage_context: Chant during ego-dissolution, Ahamkara study in Samkhya, or when the 'I' needs humbling.

meaning_en: She who is completely free from delusion and infatuation
meaning_hi: मोह (भ्रम, मूर्छा, मोहजाल) से पूर्णतः मुक्त

mohā.en: delusion, infatuation, attachment born of ignorance

modern_context: मोह = delusion/infatuation। 'गीता' का अर्जुन-विषाद मोह से था और कृष्ण ने कहा 'मोह नष्ट हुआ' (नष्टो मोहः)। मोह = 'यह शरीर ही मैं हूँ', 'यह सम्बन्ध permanent है', 'यह सम्पत्ति मेरी है'। सब भ्रम। देवी निर्मोहा सब भ्रमों से मुक्त और भक्तों को भी मुक्त करती हैं। Matrix movie में 'red pill' = निर्मोह।
modern_context_en: Moha = delusion/infatuation. Gita's Arjuna-Vishada was due to Moha and Krishna said 'Moha destroyed' (Nashto Mohah). Moha = 'this body IS me', 'this relationship is permanent', 'this wealth is mine'. All delusions. Devi Nirmoha is free from all and frees devotees too. In Matrix movie, the 'red pill' = Nirmoha.
nri_context: When the delusion abroad that material security equals spiritual safety finally breaks, when you see through the moha of permanent things in an impermanent world, Nirmohā's clarity dawns.
usage_context: Chant during Moha-dissolution, Gita Chapter 2 and 18 study, or during 'seeing through illusions' meditation.

meaning_en: She who actively destroys delusion in her devotees' minds
meaning_hi: भक्तों के मन में मोह (भ्रम) का सक्रिय रूप से नाश करने वाली
modern_context: पिछला नाम (959) कहता है देवी स्वयं मोह-मुक्त, यह कहता है वे भक्तों का मोह भी मिटाती हैं। कैसे? कभी gentle teaching से (गुरु मिलना), कभी shock therapy से (sudden loss), कभी grace से (अचानक बोध)। जब अचानक clarity आए और पुराना भ्रम टूटे, 'OMG, मैं कितना ग़लत सोच रहा था!', वह मोहनाशिनी।
modern_context_en: Previous name (959) says Devi is delusion-free; this says she destroys devotees' delusions too. How? Sometimes through gentle teaching (meeting a guru), sometimes shock therapy (sudden loss), sometimes grace (sudden realization). When sudden clarity comes and old delusion breaks, 'OMG, how wrong I was!', that is Mohanashini.
nri_context: When a sudden insight abroad shatters a long-held delusion, about a relationship, a career path, or a life philosophy, and painful clarity replaces comfortable ignorance, Mohanāśinī has done her work.
usage_context: Chant when clarity breaks through delusion, during disillusionment (which is literally de-illusion-ment = moha-nashana), or when truth replaces comfortable lies.

meaning_en: She who is free from the possessive sense of 'mine'
meaning_hi: 'मेरा' (ममत्व) भाव से पूर्णतः मुक्त
modern_context: अहंकार = 'मैं', ममता = 'मेरा'। गीता कहती है 'निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः स शान्तिम् अधिगच्छति', जो 'मेरा' और 'मैं' से मुक्त हो वह शान्ति पाता है। 'मेरा घर', 'मेरा बच्चा', 'मेरा पैसा', सब ममता। देवी निर्ममा हैं: सब कुछ उनका है लेकिन कुछ भी 'मेरा' नहीं। Sharing economy (Uber, Airbnb) = practical निर्ममता: use करो, own मत करो।
modern_context_en: Ahamkara = 'I', Mamata = 'mine'. Gita says 'Nirmamo Nirahankara Sa Shantim Adhigacchati', one free from 'mine' and 'I' attains peace. 'My house', 'my child', 'my money', all Mamata. Devi is Nirmama: everything is hers yet nothing is 'mine'. Sharing economy (Uber, Airbnb) = practical Nirmamata: use, do not own.
nri_context: When the possessive grip on things abroad, 'my house', 'my status', 'my savings' - loosens into a more fluid relationship with ownership, that shift from clutching to holding gently echoes Nirmamā's freedom.
usage_context: Chant during possessiveness-release, Gita Chapter 12 study, or during minimalism practice.

meaning_en: She who destroys the possessive attachment of 'mine' in her devotees
meaning_hi: भक्तों में 'मेरा' (ममता) की आसक्ति को नष्ट करने वाली
modern_context: पिछला नाम (961) कहता है देवी स्वयं ममता-मुक्त, यह कहता है वे भक्तों की ममता भी मिटाती हैं। कैसे? कभी loss से (जो 'मेरा' था वह गया), कभी sharing से (दूसरों को देने से ममता घटी), कभी बोध से (कुछ 'मेरा' था ही नहीं)। Marie Kondo कहती है 'does it spark joy?', देवी कहती है 'does it spark freedom?'
modern_context_en: Previous name (961) says Devi is mine-free; this says she destroys devotees' possessiveness too. How? Sometimes through loss (what was 'mine' is gone), sometimes sharing (giving reduces possessiveness), sometimes realization (nothing was ever 'mine'). Marie Kondo asks 'does it spark joy?'; Devi asks 'does it spark freedom?'
nri_context: When something 'yours' abroad is taken, a project claimed by a colleague, a friendship that drifts, possession lost in a move, the resulting lightness reveals Mamatāhantrī freeing you from the weight of 'mine'.
usage_context: Chant during loss processing, decluttering, or when the sense of 'mine' needs loosening.

meaning_en: She who is completely sinless, untouched by any transgression
meaning_hi: पूर्णतः पापरहित, किसी अपराध से अस्पर्शित
modern_context: देवी निष्पाप हैं और भक्तों को भी पाप-मुक्त करती हैं। गंगा-स्नान पाप धोता है, लेकिन गंगा स्वयं देवी। तीर्थ-यात्रा (pilgrimage) पाप-क्षालन का प्राचीन तरीक़ा है। लेकिन सबसे बड़ा तीर्थ देवी का हृदय है। जब सच्चे मन से 'माँ, मुझे माफ़ करो' बोलें, वह सबसे बड़ा pilgrimage। Confession without guilt = freedom।
modern_context_en: Devi is sinless and makes devotees sinless too. Ganga-bathing washes sins, but Ganga herself is Devi. Pilgrimage is the ancient method of sin-cleansing. But the greatest pilgrimage is Devi's heart. When sincerely saying 'Ma, forgive me', that is the greatest pilgrimage. Confession without guilt = freedom.
nri_context: When past transgressions haunt you abroad and no Ganga is nearby for ritual cleansing, sincerely turning to Niṣpāpā Devi in prayer is the most effective prayashchitta (atonement) available anywhere on earth.
usage_context: Chant during atonement, when seeking forgiveness, or during Prayashchitta meditation.

meaning_en: She who actively destroys all sins accumulated by her devotees
meaning_hi: भक्तों द्वारा संचित सभी पापों को सक्रिय रूप से नष्ट करने वाली
modern_context: पिछला नाम (963) कहता है देवी स्वयं पापमुक्त, यह कहता है वे भक्तों के पाप भी मिटाती हैं। गंगा = पापनाशिनी (इसीलिए Gangaji इतनी पूज्य), और देवी स्वयं पापनाशिनी। जब Prayag (Sangam) में स्नान करें, तीन नदियाँ (Ganga, Yamuna, Saraswati) मिलकर पाप मिटाती हैं। देवी तीनों का स्रोत हैं।
modern_context_en: Previous name (963) says Devi is sinless; this says she destroys devotees' sins too. Ganga = Papanashini (that is why Gangaji is so revered), and Devi herself is Papanashini. When bathing at Prayag (Sangam), three rivers (Ganga, Yamuna, Saraswati) together wash sins. Devi is the source of all three.
nri_context: When no sacred river is available abroad for ritual purification, invoking Pāpanāśinī Devi through sincere mantra-japa provides the same spiritual cleansing that a Ganga dip would.
usage_context: Chant during Ganga Dussehra, Kumbh Mela time, or when seeking sin-cleansing through devotion alone.

meaning_en: She who is completely free from anger
meaning_hi: क्रोध से पूर्णतः मुक्त
modern_context: Paradox: देवी दुर्गा-काली क्रोध से रक्षा करती हैं लेकिन स्वयं क्रोध-मुक्त? हाँ! उनका 'क्रोध' cosmic justice है, personal anger नहीं। जैसे judge सज़ा देता है लेकिन personally angry नहीं, देवी भी असुरों को मारती हैं लेकिन क्रोधित नहीं। 'Anger management' नहीं, 'anger transcendence'। क्रोध को manage नहीं, transcend करो।
modern_context_en: Paradox: Devi Durga-Kali seem angry but is anger-free? Yes! Their 'anger' is cosmic justice, not personal anger. Like a judge gives punishment but is not personally angry, Devi slays demons but is not wrathful. Not 'anger management' but 'anger transcendence'. Do not manage anger; transcend it.
nri_context: When rage at injustice abroad, racism, exploitation, betrayal - arises, channelling it as cosmic justice rather than personal wrath transforms destructive anger into constructive Niṣkrodhā power.
usage_context: Chant during anger transcendence, when distinguishing righteous action from personal rage, or during Krodha study in Shadripu.

meaning_en: She who pacifies and dissolves the anger within her devotees
meaning_hi: भक्तों के भीतर क्रोध को शान्त और विलीन करने वाली
modern_context: पिछला (965) कहता है देवी स्वयं क्रोध-मुक्त, यह कहता है वे भक्तों का क्रोध भी शान्त करती हैं। जब क्रोध आए, deep breathing + 'क्रोधशमनी' जाप करें। 10 seconds में decide न करें, 10 minutes wait करें। 'Response, not reaction' = क्रोधशमन। Angry email send करने से पहले draft save करें, सुबह पढ़ें, फिर decide करें।
modern_context_en: Previous (965) says Devi is anger-free; this says she pacifies devotees' anger too. When anger comes, deep breathing + 'Krodhashamani' japa. Do not decide in 10 seconds; wait 10 minutes. 'Response, not reaction' = Krodhashaman. Before sending an angry email, save the draft, read it next morning, then decide.
nri_context: When a frustrating interaction abroad triggers rage, pausing to chant 'Krodhaśamanī' internally for 10 seconds before responding transforms a potentially destructive reaction into a measured response.
usage_context: Chant during anger management, before responding to provocation, or during Shadripu (six enemies) purification.

meaning_en: She who is completely free from greed and covetousness
meaning_hi: लोभ (लालच) से पूर्णतः मुक्त
modern_context: लोभ (greed) छह शत्रुओं (षड्रिपु: काम, क्रोध, लोभ, मोह, मद, मात्सर्य) में तीसरा है। Corporate scandals (Enron, Satyam, FTX) लोभ के परिणाम। 'Greed is good' Wall Street ने कहा, लेकिन 2008 financial crisis ने disprove किया। देवी को सब कुछ available है लेकिन कोई लोभ नहीं। 'Enough' = निर्लोभ।
modern_context_en: Lobha (greed) is third among six enemies (Shadripu: Kama, Krodha, Lobha, Moha, Mada, Matsarya). Corporate scandals (Enron, Satyam, FTX) result from greed. 'Greed is good' said Wall Street but 2008's financial crisis disproved it. Everything is available to Devi yet she has no greed. 'Enough' = Nirlobha.
nri_context: When the 'more more more' culture abroad, bigger house, better car, higher salary - drives you relentlessly, Nirlobhā's greed-free contentment offers the counter-cultural wisdom: 'enough IS the feast.'
usage_context: Chant during Shadripu purification, when greed surfaces, or during ethical finance contemplation.

meaning_en: She who destroys the greed within her devotees' hearts
meaning_hi: भक्तों के हृदय में लोभ (लालच) को नष्ट करने वाली
modern_context: पिछला (967) कहता है देवी स्वयं लोभ-मुक्त, यह कहता है वे भक्तों का लोभ मिटाती हैं। कैसे? दान (giving) से। जब दूसरों को दें, लोभ कम होता है। Tithe (दसवाँ भाग दान) Christian tradition में है, Zakat Islam में, और हिन्दू धर्म में 'दानम्' यज्ञ का अंग है। Regular giving = लोभनाशन। 968 approaching 970, nearly there!
modern_context_en: Previous (967) says Devi is greed-free; this says she destroys devotees' greed too. How? Through Dana (giving). When you give to others, greed diminishes. Tithe in Christianity, Zakat in Islam, and 'Danam' in Hinduism is part of yajna. Regular giving = Lobhanashana. 968 approaching 970, nearly there!
nri_context: When establishing a regular giving practice abroad, whether 10% of income to charity, monthly donations to Indian causes, or community service, that habitual dana is Lobhanāśinī actively dissolving greed.
usage_context: Chant during Dana practice, when greed needs dissolution, or during charitable giving.

meaning_en: She who is free from all doubt and who removes doubt from the faithful
meaning_hi: सभी संशय (सन्देह) से मुक्त और विश्वासियों का संशय दूर करने वाली
modern_context: गीता कहती है 'संशयात्मा विनश्यति', सन्देह करने वाला नष्ट होता है। Doubt ज्ञान का शत्रु है। Research में 'hypothesis' होती है, doubt नहीं। Scientific doubt constructive है (verify करो), spiritual doubt destructive (faith तोड़ता है)। देवी निःसंशया हैं और भक्तों का संशय भी दूर करती हैं। जब doubt आए, 'निःसंशया' बोलें।
modern_context_en: Gita says 'Samshayatma Vinashyati', the doubter perishes. Doubt is knowledge's enemy. Research has 'hypothesis', not doubt. Scientific doubt is constructive (verify); spiritual doubt is destructive (breaks faith). Devi is Nihsamshaya and removes devotees' doubts too. When doubt comes, say 'Nihsamshaya'.
nri_context: When doubt abroad assails your faith, 'is anyone listening to my prayers?', 'does Devi even exist?', this name pierces that darkness with certainty: Niḥsaṃśayā IS, beyond all doubt.
usage_context: Chant during faith-crises, when doubt undermines practice, or during Gita Chapter 4 study on doubt's destruction.

meaning_en: She who slays all doubt with the sword of direct experience
meaning_hi: प्रत्यक्ष अनुभव की तलवार से सभी सन्देह का वध करने वाली
modern_context: पिछला (969) कहता है देवी स्वयं सन्देह-मुक्त, यह कहता है वे सन्देह का वध करती हैं। गीता कहती है 'ज्ञानासिना छिन्ध्येनं संशयम्', ज्ञान की तलवार से संशय को काटो। 'ज्ञान' = direct experience, not information। जब स्वयं अनुभव हो, doubt ख़त्म। Book-knowledge doubt नहीं मिटा सकता, experience मिटा सकता है। 970: केवल 30 नाम शेष!
modern_context_en: Previous (969) says Devi is doubt-free; this says she slays doubt. Gita says 'Jnanasina Chhindhyenam Samshayam', cut doubt with the sword of knowledge. 'Jnana' = direct experience, not information. When you experience directly, doubt ends. Book-knowledge cannot kill doubt; experience can. 970: only 30 names remain!
nri_context: When one direct experience of Devi's presence abroad, a moment of undeniable grace, a prayer answered unmistakably, a vision in meditation, destroys a lifetime of doubt, Saṃśayaghnī's sword has struck.
usage_context: Chant during doubt-crisis resolution, when seeking direct experience over theory, or during Gita Chapter 4 sword-of-knowledge meditation. A significant 970th name with only 30 remaining.

meaning_en: She who is beyond all worldly existence, transcending the cycle of birth altogether
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण सांसारिक अस्तित्व से परे, जन्म-चक्र से ही अतीत

bhavā.en: worldly existence, the cycle of becoming, samsara

modern_context: भव = becoming, संसार। देवी भव (Shiva) की पत्नी भवानी हैं, लेकिन स्वयं निर्भव (beyond samsara)। Paradox: संसार में रहकर संसार से परे। जैसे actor role में होकर भी actor है, देवी संसार चलाती हैं लेकिन संसार में बँधती नहीं। अन्तिम 30 नामों की शुरुआत इस profound teaching से: सब transcend करो।
modern_context_en: Bhava = becoming, samsara. Devi is Bhavani (wife of Bhava/Shiva) yet herself Nirbhava (beyond samsara). Paradox: in the world yet beyond it. Like an actor is in the role yet remains the actor, Devi runs the world but is not bound by it. The final 30 names begin with this profound teaching: transcend everything.
nri_context: When the cycle of immigrant striving abroad, earning, saving, planning, repeating - reveals itself as another form of bhava-chakra, this name points to the nirbhava awareness that watches the wheel without being crushed by it.
usage_context: Chant when seeking transcendence, during Samsara-mukti meditation, or at the beginning of this sacred final stretch.

meaning_en: She who destroys the cycle of worldly existence for her devotees
meaning_hi: भक्तों के लिए भव (संसार-चक्र) को नष्ट करने वाली
modern_context: पिछला (971) कहता है देवी स्वयं भव से परे, यह कहता है वे भक्तों का भव भी नष्ट करती हैं। यह paired-name pattern (Nir-X/X-Nashini) अब familiar है: देवी स्वयं मुक्त हैं और भक्तों को भी मुक्त करती हैं। भवनाशिनी = ultimate promise: जन्म-मरण चक्र ही ख़त्म। सबसे बड़ा gift: मोक्ष।
modern_context_en: Previous (971) says Devi is beyond Bhava; this says she destroys devotees' Bhava too. The paired-name pattern (Nir-X/X-Nashini) is now familiar: Devi is herself free and frees devotees. Bhavanashini = ultimate promise: ending the birth-death cycle itself. The greatest gift: Moksha.
nri_context: When the deepest longing abroad shifts from 'I want a better life' to 'I want freedom from the cycle itself', Bhavanāśinī responds to that most profound of all prayers.
usage_context: Chant when seeking ultimate liberation, during Moksha meditation, or when the birth-death cycle needs ending.

meaning_en: She who is beyond all mental alternatives, the state of choiceless awareness
meaning_hi: सभी मानसिक विकल्पों (alternatives) से परे, विकल्परहित जागरूकता

vikalpā.en: alternatives, mental constructs, conceptual thought, either-or thinking

modern_context: निर्विकल्प समाधि = Samadhi without any mental construct। यह Patanjali Yoga Sutra का सर्वोच्च goal है। Savikalpa samadhi में 'मैं ध्यान कर रहा हूँ' का भान रहता है, Nirvikalpa में कोई भान नहीं, केवल Being। 'This or that?' का प्रश्न ही विलीन। जब meditation में 'ध्यान कर रहा हूँ' भी भूल जाएँ, वही निर्विकल्प।
modern_context_en: Nirvikalpa Samadhi = Samadhi without any mental construct. This is Patanjali Yoga Sutra's highest goal. In Savikalpa Samadhi, awareness of 'I am meditating' remains; in Nirvikalpa, no awareness of anything, only Being. The question 'this or that?' dissolves entirely. When in meditation even 'I am meditating' is forgotten, that is Nirvikalpa.
nri_context: When every choice abroad, career A or B, city X or Y, stay or return - collapses into a choiceless clarity where the right path simply reveals itself without deliberation, you touch Nirvikalpā.
usage_context: Chant during Nirvikalpa Samadhi aspiration, when choice-paralysis dissolves, or during Yoga Sutra's highest meditation.

meaning_en: She who is free from all obstruction and who cannot be hindered by any force
meaning_hi: सभी बाधाओं से मुक्त, किसी शक्ति द्वारा रोकी न जा सकने वाली
modern_context: देवी की गति को कोई रोक नहीं सकता। Unstoppable force = Nirabadha। जब river बाँध तोड़े, जब volcano फूटे, जब tsunami आए, कोई नहीं रोक सकता। देवी उससे भी अधिक unstoppable। जब आप right path पर हों और obstacles आएँ, जानें: देवी निराबाधा हैं और आपका भी रास्ता clear करेंगी।
modern_context_en: Nothing can stop Devi's movement. Unstoppable force = Nirabadha. When a river breaks a dam, a volcano erupts, a tsunami comes, nothing can stop it. Devi is even more unstoppable. When you are on the right path and obstacles come, know: Devi is Nirabadha and will clear your path too.
nri_context: When bureaucratic obstacles abroad seem insurmountable, when the system itself appears designed to block you, Nirābādhā's unstoppable energy reminds: no force in the universe can permanently obstruct what Devi wills.
usage_context: Chant when facing insurmountable obstacles, during breakthrough prayers, or when unstoppable momentum is needed.

meaning_en: She in whom there is no division, difference, or separation of any kind
meaning_hi: जिनमें कोई भेद, विभाजन या पृथकता नहीं
modern_context: भेद = division। जाति-भेद, लिंग-भेद, धर्म-भेद, देश-भेद सब मनुष्य-निर्मित। देवी निर्भेद हैं: उनमें कोई division नहीं। 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' (सम्पूर्ण विश्व एक परिवार) निर्भेद-भाव है। UN's 'we the peoples' भी निर्भेद आदर्श। जब सब भेद मिटें और एकता दिखे, वही निर्भेद दर्शन।
modern_context_en: Bheda = division. Caste-division, gender-division, religion-division, nation-division are all human-made. Devi is Nirbheda: no division within her. 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' (the whole world is one family) is the Nirbheda spirit. UN's 'we the peoples' is also a Nirbheda ideal. When all divisions vanish and unity appears, that is Nirbheda darshan.
nri_context: When the artificial divisions of the world abroad, race, nationality, religion, class - dissolve in a moment of genuine human connection, Nirbhedā's undivided reality shines through.
usage_context: Chant during unity meditation, interfaith gatherings, or when divisions need dissolving.

meaning_en: She who destroys all sense of division and separateness
meaning_hi: भेद (विभाजन, पृथकता) की भावना को नष्ट करने वाली
modern_context: देवी भेद (division) को actively नष्ट करती हैं। जब शिक्षा casteism मिटाए, जब inter-caste marriage भेद तोड़े, जब interfaith dialogue दीवारें गिराए, सब भेदनाशिनी का कार्य। Dr. Ambedkar जिन्होंने caste-division तोड़ी, Mahatma Gandhi जिन्होंने Hindu-Muslim एकता का प्रयास किया, सब भेदनाशिनी शक्ति से।
modern_context_en: Devi actively destroys division. When education removes casteism, inter-caste marriage breaks barriers, interfaith dialogue demolishes walls, all are Bhedanashini's work. Dr. Ambedkar who broke caste-division, Mahatma Gandhi who attempted Hindu-Muslim unity, all through Bhedanashini's power.
nri_context: When your friendships abroad naturally cross every line of nationality, race, and religion, and you no longer see 'others' but only 'us', Bhedanāśinī has destroyed the divisions in your consciousness.
usage_context: Chant during anti-discrimination efforts, unity building, or during Advaita (non-duality) meditation.

meaning_en: She who is indestructible, beyond all decay and annihilation
meaning_hi: अविनाशी, सभी क्षय और विनाश से परे
modern_context: सब कुछ नश्वर (destructible) है: empires गिरे, species extinct हुईं, stars die करते हैं। लेकिन देवी अविनाशी। Second Law of Thermodynamics (entropy increases) सब पर लागू, देवी पर नहीं। जब सब कुछ नष्ट हो (Big Crunch), देवी बचेंगी। और फिर से सृष्टि करेंगी। Eternal recurrence = Nirnasha Devi।
modern_context_en: Everything is destructible: empires fell, species went extinct, stars die. But Devi is indestructible. Second Law of Thermodynamics applies to all except Devi. When everything is destroyed (Big Crunch), Devi will remain. And create again. Eternal recurrence = Nirnasha Devi.
nri_context: When everything built abroad seems fragile and temporary, career, health, relationships, all subject to destruction, connecting to Nirnāśā's indestructible nature provides the one anchor that cannot be taken away.
usage_context: Chant during impermanence contemplation, when mortality looms, or during the eternal-amid-transient meditation.

meaning_en: She who churns and destroys death itself
meaning_hi: स्वयं मृत्यु को मथकर नष्ट करने वाली
modern_context: मृत्यु को भी नष्ट करने वाली! Mahamrityunjaya Mantra ('मृत्युञ्जय' = death-conqueror) शिव का है, लेकिन शक्ति (देवी) उसके पीछे की energy। जब Markandeya को Yama ने पकड़ा, शिव ने बचाया, लेकिन शक्ति के बिना शिव भी यह न कर पाते। Anti-ageing science मृत्यु से लड़ रही है, लेकिन असली मृत्युमथनी देवी हैं: spiritual death = liberation from death।
modern_context_en: She who destroys even death! Mahamrityunjaya Mantra ('Mrityunjaya' = death-conqueror) is Shiva's, but Shakti (Devi) is the energy behind it. When Yama seized Markandeya, Shiva saved him, but without Shakti, Shiva could not. Anti-ageing science fights death, but the real Mrityumathani is Devi: spiritual death of death = liberation.
nri_context: When the fear of dying alone abroad, far from family, from sacred rivers, from homeland - surfaces, Mṛtyumathanī crushes death's power itself. She who defeated death will not let death defeat her devotee.
usage_context: Chant during Mahamrityunjaya japa, death-anxiety, or during Markandeya Purana study. One of the most powerful names in the final stretch.

meaning_en: She who is actionless, achieving everything without any effort or doing
meaning_hi: क्रियारहित, बिना किसी प्रयास या कर्म के सब कुछ सिद्ध करने वाली
modern_context: देवी सब करती हैं लेकिन कुछ 'करती' नहीं। Sun कुछ 'करता' नहीं, बस 'है', और उसके होने से सब कुछ होता है: दिन, रात, मौसम, जीवन। देवी भी बस 'हैं' और उनके होने से ब्रह्माण्ड चलता है। Wu Wei (Tao) = actionless action = निष्क्रिया। सबसे efficient action = no forced action।
modern_context_en: Devi does everything yet 'does' nothing. The sun does not 'do' anything; it just IS, and from its being everything happens: day, night, seasons, life. Devi just IS and from her being the cosmos runs. Wu Wei (Tao) = actionless action = Nishkriya. The most efficient action = no forced action.
nri_context: When you stop forcing outcomes abroad and instead align with the natural flow, and things begin happening effortlessly, that wu-wei state is Niṣkriyā's actionless action working through your surrender.
usage_context: Chant during effortless action practice, Wu Wei meditation, or during Gita's Akarma (actionlessness in action) study.

meaning_en: She who possesses nothing yet owns everything, free from all accumulation
meaning_hi: कुछ भी न रखते हुए सब कुछ की स्वामिनी, सभी संग्रह से मुक्त
modern_context: अपरिग्रह (non-accumulation) Patanjali Yoga Sutra में पाँचवाँ Yama है। Gandhi जी ने कहा 'Earth provides enough for every man's need but not every man's greed'। Minimalism movement, zero-waste lifestyle, और decluttering सब अपरिग्रह हैं। देवी जिनके पास सब कुछ है, कुछ 'रखती' नहीं। Flowing river = अपरिग्रह, stagnant pond = परिग्रह।
modern_context_en: Aparigraha (non-accumulation) is Patanjali's fifth Yama. Gandhi said 'Earth provides enough for every man's need but not every man's greed.' Minimalism, zero-waste lifestyle, decluttering are all Aparigraha. Devi who has everything 'keeps' nothing. Flowing river = Aparigraha; stagnant pond = Parigraha.
nri_context: When you begin releasing accumulated possessions abroad, donating what is not needed, simplifying lifestyle, letting go of hoarded things, Niṣparigrahā's freedom from accumulation flows through your open hands.
usage_context: Chant during Aparigraha practice, minimalism, decluttering, or during Yoga Sutra Yama study.

meaning_en: She who is incomparable, matchless, beyond all comparison
meaning_hi: अतुलनीय, बेजोड़, सभी तुलना से परे
modern_context: निस्तुला = beyond comparison। 'X is better than Y' सोचना मानव-स्वभाव है लेकिन देवी किसी comparison में नहीं आतीं। Google search 'best God' = no result, because God is Nistula। Forbes list richest person rank करती है लेकिन देवी unrankable। Comparison is the thief of joy (Theodore Roosevelt), देवी comparison से परे = joy itself।
modern_context_en: Nistula = beyond comparison. Thinking 'X is better than Y' is human nature but Devi fits no comparison. Google search 'best God' = no result because God is Nistula. Forbes lists rank the richest but Devi is unrankable. 'Comparison is the thief of joy' (Theodore Roosevelt); Devi beyond comparison = joy itself.
nri_context: When the comparison trap abroad, comparing yourself to more successful immigrants, richer neighbours, higher achievers - steals your peace, Nistulā whispers: you too are incomparable. Stop comparing.
usage_context: Chant when comparison steals joy, during self-acceptance, or when uniqueness needs celebrating.

meaning_en: She who has lustrous dark blue-black hair like the night sky
meaning_hi: रात्रि-आकाश जैसे चमकदार गहरे नीले-काले केश वाली

nīla.en: dark blue-black, the colour of the night sky

modern_context: अन्तिम 20 नामों में physical description लौटता है: केश (hair) से शुरू। देवी के बाल रात के आकाश जैसे गहरे नीले-काले हैं। भारतीय सौन्दर्यशास्त्र में काले बाल सबसे सुन्दर माने जाते हैं। Neem oil, Amla, Bhringraj traditional hair-care हैं जो बालों को नील (dark) रखते हैं। देवी के केश cosmic hair हैं: Milky Way एक लट है।
modern_context_en: In the final 20 names, physical description returns: starting with hair. Devi's hair is night-sky dark blue-black. In Indian aesthetics, dark hair is considered most beautiful. Neem oil, Amla, Bhringraj are traditional hair-care keeping hair Neela (dark). Devi's hair is cosmic: the Milky Way is one strand.
nri_context: When maintaining traditional hair-care abroad, oiling with coconut and amla, braiding before bed, your dark tresses carry a whisper of Nīlacikurā's cosmic beauty through ancient practice.
usage_context: Chant during hair-care rituals, Devi's physical-form meditation, or when celebrating Indian beauty traditions.

meaning_en: She from whom there is no falling away, the one who never diminishes
meaning_hi: जिनसे कोई पतन (अपाय) नहीं, जो कभी कम नहीं होतीं
modern_context: देवी में कोई ह्रास (diminution) नहीं: उनका प्रेम कम नहीं होता, उनकी शक्ति घटती नहीं, उनकी कृपा सूखती नहीं। जब माँ 10 बच्चों को प्रेम दे, 10वें को कम नहीं मिलता। देवी अरबों भक्तों को कृपा दें, किसी को कम नहीं। Abundance mindset = Nirapaya: कभी कम नहीं होगा, बस बढ़ेगा।
modern_context_en: Devi has no diminution: her love does not decrease, her power does not wane, her grace does not dry up. When a mother loves 10 children, the 10th does not get less. Devi blesses billions of devotees; none gets less. Abundance mindset = Nirapaya: it will never decrease, only increase.
