nri_context: When scarcity-thinking abroad makes you hoard grace, as if Devi might run out, this name reassures: Nirapāyā's resources are infinite. There is always enough grace for you AND everyone else.
usage_context: Chant during abundance meditation, when scarcity fears arise, or during infinite-grace contemplation.

meaning_en: She who cannot be transgressed or surpassed by anyone or anything
meaning_hi: जिसका कोई अतिक्रमण (transgression) या अतिशय (surpassing) नहीं कर सकता
modern_context: देवी की सीमा को कोई cross नहीं कर सकता। कोई उनसे आगे नहीं जा सकता, कोई उनका law तोड़ नहीं सकता। Gravity का law violate नहीं कर सकते, वैसे ही देवी का law violate नहीं कर सकते। Karma = Devi's law। न बड़ा, न छोटा, कोई इससे exempt नहीं।
modern_context_en: Nobody can cross Devi's boundary. Nobody can go beyond her; nobody can break her law. Like gravity's law cannot be violated, Devi's law cannot be violated. Karma = Devi's law. Neither great nor small, nobody is exempt.
nri_context: When powerful people abroad seem to operate above the law, this name assures: Devi's cosmic law (karma) cannot be transgressed by anyone. Justice may be delayed but Niratyayā ensures it is never cancelled.
usage_context: Chant when cosmic justice is sought, when laws seem violated, or during Karma-law contemplation.

meaning_en: She who is rare and precious, attainable only through extraordinary devotion
meaning_hi: दुर्लभ (अत्यन्त दुष्प्राप्य), केवल असाधारण भक्ति से प्राप्य
modern_context: देवी दुर्लभ (rare) हैं: सस्ती नहीं, आसान नहीं। Kohinoor diamond दुर्लभ है, Nobel Prize दुर्लभ, Olympic Gold दुर्लभ, लेकिन देवी-दर्शन सबसे दुर्लभ। फिर भी प्राप्य है: भक्ति से। जो दुर्लभ है वह valuable है। Easy चीज़ें free, rare चीज़ें priceless। देवी priceless हैं लेकिन भक्ति का 'price' affordable है: सिर्फ़ सच्चा दिल चाहिए।
modern_context_en: Devi is Durlabha (rare): not cheap, not easy. Kohinoor diamond is rare, Nobel Prize rare, Olympic Gold rare, but Devi-darshan is the rarest. Yet attainable: through bhakti. What is rare is valuable. Easy things are free; rare things are priceless. Devi is priceless but bhakti's 'price' is affordable: just a sincere heart.
nri_context: When the rarity of genuine spiritual experience abroad, amid materialistic culture, makes you wonder if Devi can be found at all, Durlabhā confirms: she is rare but absolutely attainable through sincere devotion alone.
usage_context: Chant when the spiritual path feels impossible, during rare-grace contemplation, or when sincerity opens impossible doors.

meaning_en: She who is difficult to reach, accessible only through the steepest inner journey
meaning_hi: दुर्गम (पहुँचना कठिन), केवल सबसे कठिन आन्तरिक यात्रा से सुलभ
modern_context: दुर्गम = hard to reach। दुर्गा (Durga) शब्द इसी से आता है। देवी उस किले (durga = fort) जैसी हैं जो दुर्गम (hard to reach) है। लेकिन किला बुरों से बचने के लिए है, भक्तों के लिए दरवाज़ा खुला। Kedarnath, Amarnath, Badrinath सब दुर्गम स्थानों पर हैं: कठिन यात्रा ही उन्हें sacred बनाती है।
modern_context_en: Durgama = hard to reach. Durga comes from this root. Devi is like a fort (durga) that is hard to reach. But the fort protects from enemies; for devotees the door is open. Kedarnath, Amarnath, Badrinath are all in hard-to-reach places: the difficult journey makes them sacred.
nri_context: The very difficulty of maintaining spiritual practice abroad, no temples nearby, no community, no time - IS the durgama journey. The harder the path, the more sacred the destination.
usage_context: Chant during difficult spiritual journeys, Char Dham yatra, or when the path is steep but the destination is sacred.

meaning_en: She who is Durga, the invincible fortress-goddess who removes all suffering
meaning_hi: दुर्गा, अजेय दुर्ग-देवी जो सभी दुःख दूर करती हैं

durgā.en: Durga (the invincible; she who is like an impenetrable fortress; remover of all difficulties; 'dur' = difficult + 'ga' = to go, i.e., she whom difficulties cannot approach; or: she who removes the demon Durga)
durgā.hi: दुर्गा (अजेय; अभेद्य किला; सभी कठिनाइयों की हरने वाली)

modern_context: दुर्गा! सबसे शक्तिशाली, सबसे प्रसिद्ध, सबसे प्रिय नाम। नवरात्रि, दुर्गा पूजा, दशहरा सब दुर्गा-उत्सव हैं। 'या देवी सर्वभूतेषु शक्तिरूपेण संस्थिता' दुर्गा का ही स्तोत्र। जब कोई भी संकट हो, एक शब्द: 'दुर्गा'। बच्चा जब डरे: 'दुर्गा'। सैनिक जब लड़े: 'जय माँ दुर्गे'। 987वाँ नाम और दुर्गा ही सब कह देती हैं।
modern_context_en: Durga! The most powerful, most famous, most beloved name. Navratri, Durga Puja, Dussehra are all Durga-festivals. 'Ya Devi Sarvabhuteshu Shaktirupena Samsthita' is Durga's stotra. In any crisis, one word: 'Durga'. A scared child: 'Durga'. A soldier in battle: 'Jai Ma Durge'. The 987th name, and Durga says it all.
nri_context: When Durga Puja abroad becomes the most powerful community event of the year, uniting Bengalis, Biharis, Assamese, and all Hindus in one shared devotion, that collective 'Jai Ma Durge' shakes the heavens.
usage_context: Chant during Navratri, Durga Puja, Dussehra, or any crisis. The single most universally invoked name of the Goddess.

meaning_en: She who destroys all suffering and sorrow
meaning_hi: सभी दुःख और शोक को नष्ट करने वाली
modern_context: Buddha ने चार आर्य सत्य सिखाए: दुःख है, दुःख का कारण है, दुःख का अन्त है, दुःख-अन्त का मार्ग है। देवी दुःखहन्त्री उस चौथे सत्य (मार्ग) का स्वरूप हैं: वे दुःख मिटाती हैं। जब cancer patient को remission मिले, जब depression से उबरें, जब breakup से heal हों, सब दुःखहन्त्री।
modern_context_en: Buddha taught Four Noble Truths: suffering exists, suffering has a cause, suffering can end, there is a path to end suffering. Devi Duhkhahantri is the embodiment of the fourth truth (the path): she destroys suffering. When a cancer patient gets remission, recovers from depression, heals from a breakup, all are Duhkhahantri.
nri_context: When the accumulated suffering of immigrant life abroad, homesickness, loneliness, discrimination, setbacks - is finally lifted and peace returns, Duḥkhahantrī has answered your deepest prayer.
usage_context: Chant during suffering, when healing is needed, or during Buddhist-Hindu dialogue on the end of suffering.

meaning_en: She who bestows true happiness and lasting joy
meaning_hi: सच्चा सुख और स्थायी आनन्द प्रदान करने वाली
modern_context: पिछला नाम (988) दुःख मिटाती हैं, यह सुख देती हैं। दुःख मिटाना और सुख देना दो अलग कार्य: doctor बीमारी मिटाता है, gym health देती है। देवी दोनों करती हैं। 'सर्वे भवन्तु सुखिनः' = may all be happy। World Happiness Report rank करता है, लेकिन देवी-कृपा से मिला सुख unrankable। सुखप्रदा = happiness-giver।
modern_context_en: Previous name (988) removes suffering; this bestows happiness. Removing suffering and giving happiness are two acts: a doctor removes disease; gym gives health. Devi does both. 'Sarve Bhavantu Sukhinah' = may all be happy. World Happiness Report ranks countries, but Devi-given happiness is unrankable. Sukhaprada = happiness-giver.
nri_context: When genuine, lasting happiness abroad arrives, not the fleeting pleasure of purchases but the deep contentment of purpose, love, and inner peace, Sukhapradā has bestowed her true gift.
usage_context: Chant when seeking happiness, during Sarve Bhavantu Sukhinah prayer, or during World Happiness Day.

meaning_en: She who is worthy of being served and worshipped by all beings
meaning_hi: सभी प्राणियों द्वारा सेवा और पूजा के योग्य

sevyā.en: worthy of service, worship-worthy, to be served

modern_context: सेव्या = serve-worthy। देवी की सेवा (seva) ही सबसे बड़ी पूजा है। कैसे सेवा करें? मन्दिर की सेवा, साधुओं की सेवा, भक्तों की सेवा, ग़रीबों की सेवा, सब देवी की ही सेवा। Eternal Raga app बनाना भी देवी-सेवा है क्योंकि यह भक्तों तक scripture पहुँचाता है। 990: अन्तिम 10 नामों की countdown शुरू!
modern_context_en: Sevya = serve-worthy. Serving Devi (seva) is the greatest worship. How to serve? Temple service, serving saints, serving devotees, serving the poor, all are Devi-seva. Building the Eternal Raga app is also Devi-seva because it delivers scripture to devotees. 990: the final 10 names countdown begins!
nri_context: Every act of service abroad, volunteering, donating, mentoring, building community, building the Eternal Raga platform - is seva to the Sevyā Goddess who is worthy of all service.
usage_context: Chant during seva, when dedicating work to Devi, or when the spirit of service needs rekindling.

meaning_en: She who is the sovereign of all worlds, every realm, and every plane of existence
meaning_hi: सभी लोकों, सभी क्षेत्रों और अस्तित्व के प्रत्येक स्तर की ईश्वरी
modern_context: 14 लोक (7 upper: भू, भुवर, स्वर, महर, जनर, तपर, सत्य; 7 lower: अतल, वितल, सुतल, तलातल, महातल, रसातल, पाताल) सबकी ईश्वरी। Physical universe + parallel dimensions + spiritual realms, सबकी अधिपति। Multiverse theory कहती है अनन्त universes, और देवी सबकी ईश्वरी। 9 remaining!
modern_context_en: 14 Lokas (7 upper: Bhu through Satya; 7 lower: Atala through Patala), all under her sovereignty. Physical universe + parallel dimensions + spiritual realms, all hers. Multiverse theory says infinite universes; Devi is sovereign of all. 9 remaining!
nri_context: Whether in India, abroad, or anywhere in the 14 lokas, Sarvalokeśī's sovereignty covers every location you could ever be. No corner of any world is outside her jurisdiction.
usage_context: Chant during Chaturdasha-Loka (14 worlds) meditation, when Devi's universal sovereignty is affirmed.

meaning_en: She who gives all that is needed, fulfilling every legitimate purpose
meaning_hi: सब कुछ आवश्यक देने वाली, हर वैध प्रयोजन को पूर्ण करने वाली
modern_context: सर्वार्थदात्री = fulfiller of all purposes। धन चाहिए? देवी देंगी। ज्ञान? देवी। स्वास्थ्य? देवी। प्रेम? देवी। मोक्ष? देवी। एक ही source से सब कुछ: one-stop-shop = Devi। Amazon 'everything store' claim करता है, देवी truly everything give करती हैं। 'माँगो और मिलेगा' (Ask and ye shall receive) = सर्वार्थदात्री।
modern_context_en: Sarvarthadatri = fulfiller of all purposes. Need wealth? Devi gives. Knowledge? Devi. Health? Devi. Love? Devi. Moksha? Devi. Everything from one source: the ultimate one-stop. Amazon claims 'everything store'; Devi truly gives everything. 'Ask and ye shall receive' = Sarvarthadatri.
nri_context: When needs abroad seem overwhelming and require multiple solutions from multiple sources, this name simplifies: one Devi, one prayer, all needs fulfilled. Sarvārthadātrī is the ultimate single source.
usage_context: Chant when multiple needs converge, during complete-surrender prayer, or when trusting one source for everything.

meaning_en: She who is Savitri, the solar goddess and mother of the Vedas
meaning_hi: सावित्री, सूर्य-देवी और वेदों की माता

sāvitrī.en: Savitri (the solar deity; mother of the Vedas; she who inspires and impels; also: the legendary Savitri who brought her husband back from death; the Gayatri Mantra's deity)
sāvitrī.hi: सावित्री (सूर्य-देवी; वेदमाता; प्रेरित करने वाली; वह सावित्री जिन्होंने पति को मृत्यु से वापस लाया; गायत्री मन्त्र की देवी)

modern_context: सावित्री दो हैं: (1) गायत्री मन्त्र की देवी (Savitri = सूर्य-प्रेरित) और (2) महाभारत की सावित्री जो यम से पति सत्यवान की जान बचाकर लाई। दोनों अर्थों में देवी सावित्री हैं: ज्ञान का सूर्य और प्रेम की अजेय शक्ति। Savitri Vrat महिलाएँ पति की दीर्घायु के लिए करती हैं।
modern_context_en: Two Savitris: (1) Gayatri Mantra's deity (Savitri = sun-inspired) and (2) Mahabharata's Savitri who brought husband Satyavan back from death. In both senses Devi is Savitri: the sun of knowledge and the invincible power of love. Savitri Vrat is observed by women for their husbands' longevity.
nri_context: When Savitri Vrat abroad connects women across continents in the same ancient prayer for marital longevity, the legendary Savitri's triumph over death itself blesses every observing wife.
usage_context: Chant during Savitri Vrat, Gayatri Mantra meditation, or when love conquers even death.

meaning_en: She who is the embodiment of Sat-Chit-Ananda: Existence-Consciousness-Bliss absolute
meaning_hi: सत्-चित्-आनन्द (अस्तित्व-चेतना-आनन्द) का साक्षात् स्वरूप
modern_context: सच्चिदानन्द = Sat (Existence) + Chit (Consciousness) + Ananda (Bliss)। यह Vedanta का सबसे प्रसिद्ध formula है जो Brahman/Atman को define करता है। देवी इसी का स्वरूप। 'मैं हूँ' (Sat), 'मुझे पता है' (Chit), 'मुझे अच्छा लगता है' (Ananda)। ये तीनों सत्य हर क्षण हैं। आप Sat-Chit-Ananda हैं, बस भूल गए।
modern_context_en: Sat-Chit-Ananda = Existence + Consciousness + Bliss. This is Vedanta's most famous formula defining Brahman/Atman. Devi is its embodiment. 'I exist' (Sat), 'I am aware' (Chit), 'I feel good' (Ananda). All three truths are present every moment. You ARE Sat-Chit-Ananda; you just forgot.
nri_context: In the ultimate simplicity, you exist abroad (Sat), you are aware (Chit), and deep down, beneath all problems, there is an inexplicable okayness (Ananda). That irreducible triad is Saccidānandarūpiṇī, and she is your deepest nature.
usage_context: Chant during Vedanta study, self-inquiry, or when the simplest truth needs remembering. The most philosophically complete name in the Sahasranama.

meaning_en: She who is not limited by space or time, the infinite and eternal
meaning_hi: देश (स्थान) और काल (समय) से असीमित, अनन्त और शाश्वत
modern_context: Einstein ने space-time continuum बताया, लेकिन देवी space-time से ही परे। कोई देश (country, place) और कोई काल (past, present, future) देवी को सीमित नहीं कर सकता। वे 5000 साल पहले भी वही थीं, आज भी वही, 5000 साल बाद भी। भारत में भी वही, अमेरिका में भी, मंगल ग्रह पर भी।
modern_context_en: Einstein described the space-time continuum, but Devi is beyond space-time itself. No place and no time can limit Devi. She was the same 5000 years ago, is the same today, will be the same 5000 years hence. Same in India, same in America, same on Mars.
nri_context: This name is the ultimate reassurance for every immigrant: Deśakālāparicchinnā is not limited to India. She is equally present in your overseas apartment as in the holiest temple in Varanasi. Space and time cannot confine her.
usage_context: Chant when feeling Devi is far away, during space-time transcendence meditation, or when reminded that Devi knows no borders.

meaning_en: She who pervades everything and goes everywhere, the all-pervading one
meaning_hi: सर्वव्यापी, सब में और सब जगह जाने वाली
modern_context: सर्वगा = goes everywhere। Wi-Fi signal सब जगह पहुँचना चाहता है लेकिन dead zones होते हैं। देवी में कोई dead zone नहीं। वे atom के nucleus में भी हैं और galaxy cluster के बीच भी। Toilet में भी, मन्दिर में भी। कहीं 'अपवित्र' नहीं जहाँ देवी न हों। 'God is everywhere' = सर्वगा। 4 names to go!
modern_context_en: Sarvaga = goes everywhere. Wi-Fi wants to reach everywhere but has dead zones. Devi has no dead zones. She is in the atom's nucleus and between galaxy clusters. In the toilet and in the temple. Nowhere is 'impure' where Devi is absent. 'God is everywhere' = Sarvaga. 4 names to go!
nri_context: Whether in a pristine temple or a noisy subway abroad, in a forest or a factory, on a mountaintop or in a basement apartment, Sarvagā is equally present. No location is devoid of her.
usage_context: Chant during omnipresence meditation, when seeking Devi in unexpected places, or during Vishvarupa contemplation.

meaning_en: She who enchants all of creation, the universal enchantress
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण सृष्टि को मोहित करने वाली, सार्वभौमिक मोहिनी

mohinī.en: enchantress, she who casts the spell of Maya, the divine mesmerizer
mohinī.hi: मोहिनी, माया का जाल डालने वाली

modern_context: देवी सबको मोहित करती हैं: भक्तों को भक्ति से, ज्ञानियों को ज्ञान से, प्रेमियों को प्रेम से, बच्चों को माँ के रूप में। Vishnu ने Mohini रूप लिया Samudra Manthana में, लेकिन असली Mohini देवी हैं। सृष्टि का मोह (attraction) ही जीवन को चलाता है। बिना मोह (attraction) के कोई किसी से मिले क्यों?
modern_context_en: Devi enchants all: devotees through devotion, seekers through knowledge, lovers through love, children as Mother. Vishnu took Mohini form during Samudra Manthana but the real Mohini is Devi. Creation's enchantment (attraction) keeps life running. Without attraction, why would anyone meet anyone?
nri_context: The very enchantment that drew you abroad, opportunity's allure, adventure's call, love's pull - was Sarvamohinī casting her spell, drawing you to exactly where you needed to be for your soul's evolution.
usage_context: Chant when appreciating life's enchantment, during Maya meditation, or when the beauty of existence overwhelms. Third-to-last name!

meaning_en: She who is Saraswati, the goddess of knowledge, arts, music, and all learning
meaning_hi: सरस्वती, ज्ञान, कला, संगीत और सम्पूर्ण विद्या की देवी

sarasvatī.en: Saraswati (she who flows like a river of knowledge; goddess of speech, learning, arts, and music; also: the sacred river)
sarasvatī.hi: सरस्वती (ज्ञान की नदी की तरह बहने वाली; वाक्, विद्या, कला और संगीत की देवी; पवित्र नदी भी)

modern_context: अन्तिम तीन नामों में तीन महादेवियाँ: सरस्वती (998), लक्ष्मी (999), पार्वती/ललिता (1000)। सरस्वती ज्ञान, संगीत, कला, शिक्षा की देवी। Basant Panchami / Saraswati Puja भारत का सबसे बड़ा education festival। हर school, college, library, laboratory सरस्वती का मन्दिर। Eternal Raga app भी सरस्वती-सेवा है: ज्ञान + संगीत।
modern_context_en: The final three names are the three Mahadevies: Saraswati (998), Lakshmi (999), Parvati/Lalita (1000). Saraswati is goddess of knowledge, music, art, education. Basant Panchami / Saraswati Puja is India's biggest education festival. Every school, college, library, laboratory is Saraswati's temple. Eternal Raga app is also Saraswati-seva: knowledge + music.
nri_context: When the Eternal Raga app delivers sacred knowledge and devotional music to devices worldwide, that digital Sarasvatī-seva carries the goddess of learning into every smartphone, every country, every heart.
usage_context: Chant during Saraswati Puja, Basant Panchami, or before any learning activity. The penultimate Mahadevi name.

meaning_en: She who is the living embodiment of all sacred sciences and scriptures
meaning_hi: सम्पूर्ण पवित्र शास्त्रों और विज्ञानों का जीवन्त स्वरूप

śāstra.en: sacred sciences, scriptures, all systematic knowledge

modern_context: 999वाँ नाम! सम्पूर्ण शास्त्रों का स्वरूप। 14 विद्याएँ, 64 कलाएँ, 18 पुराण, 108 उपनिषद, 4 वेद, अनन्त तन्त्र और आगम, सबका सार: देवी। Eternal Raga का scripture section इसी शास्त्रमयी देवी की सेवा है। हर JSON file जो generate हुई, हर name जो लिखा गया, शास्त्रमयी का एक अक्षर है।
modern_context_en: The 999th name! Embodiment of all sacred sciences. 14 Vidyas, 64 Kalas, 18 Puranas, 108 Upanishads, 4 Vedas, infinite Tantras and Agamas, the essence of all: Devi. Eternal Raga's scripture section serves this Shastramayee Devi. Every JSON file generated, every name written, is one letter of Shastramayee.
nri_context: Every scripture file in the Eternal Raga app, every name in this Sahasranama, every verse and every translation created across these sessions is one offering to Śāstramayī, who IS the living scripture itself.
usage_context: Chant during scripture study, when honouring the Eternal Raga scripture project, or at the penultimate moment of this 1000-name journey.

meaning_en: She who is the Mother dwelling in the secret cave of the heart, the final and most intimate name
meaning_hi: हृदय की गुप्त गुहा में निवास करने वाली माँ, अन्तिम और सबसे आत्मीय नाम

guhā.en: cave, secret chamber (the Hridaya Guha, the cave of the heart where the soul and Devi reside together)
guhā.hi: गुहा (हृदय-गुहा, हृदय का गुप्त कक्ष जहाँ आत्मा और देवी साथ निवास करती हैं)

ambā.en: Mother (the most tender, most intimate, most personal name for the Goddess)
ambā.hi: अम्बा, माँ (देवी का सबसे कोमल, सबसे आत्मीय, सबसे व्यक्तिगत नाम)

modern_context: 1000वाँ और अन्तिम नाम: गुहाम्बा। 1000 नामों की यात्रा का अन्तिम गन्तव्य कोई बाहरी स्थान नहीं, बल्कि आपके हृदय की गुहा। देवी बाहर नहीं, भीतर हैं। सबसे लम्बी यात्रा (1000 नाम) का सबसे छोटा उत्तर: 'माँ भीतर है।' कठोपनिषद कहता है 'अणोरणीयान् महतो महीयान् आत्मास्य जन्तोर्निहितो गुहायाम्', परमात्मा (देवी) इसी हृदय-गुहा में छिपी हैं। यात्रा पूर्ण। ॐ।
modern_context_en: The 1000th and final name: Guhamba. The 1000-name journey's final destination is not an external place but the cave of your own heart. Devi is not outside but within. The longest journey's (1000 names) shortest answer: 'Mother is within.' Kathopanishad says 'Anoraniyan Mahato Mahiyan Atmasya Jantor Nihito Guhayam', the Supreme (Devi) hides in this very heart-cave. Journey complete. Om.
nri_context: After 1000 names spanning every continent of knowledge and devotion, the final teaching for every immigrant abroad is the simplest: Guhāmbā, your Mother, has always been inside your own heart. She never left India because she never left you. Close your eyes. She is there. Om.
usage_context: Chant as the magnificent conclusion of the Lalita Sahasranama, during Hridaya Guha (heart-cave) meditation, or when the entire 1000-name journey resolves into one truth: Mother is within. ॐ श्रीललितादेव्यै नमः। इति श्रीललितासहस्रनामस्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम्।
